# **Trump warns European allies he no longer feels obligated to "think purely of Peace" amid intentions to acquire Greenland**
President Donald Trump **[sent a message to Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre](https://substack.com/redirect/419af5dc-9567-4f75-aa4a-186d96c1f248?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that was forwarded to multiple European ambassadors saying "Considering your Country decided not to give me the Nobel Peace Prize for having stopped 8 Wars PLUS, I no longer feel an obligation to think purely of Peace, although it will always be predominant, but can now think about what is good and proper for the United States of America."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/419af5dc-9567-4f75-aa4a-186d96c1f248?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump demands NATO help U.S. gain "Complete and Total Control of Greenland":** In his message, Trump argued "Denmark cannot protect that land from Russia or China" and questioned Denmark's "right of ownership," stating "I have done more for NATO than any other person since its founding, and now, NATO should do something for the United States. The World is not secure unless we have Complete and Total Control of Greenland," while separately threatening 10% tariffs on eight European nations including Denmark unless Copenhagen agreed to the "complete and total purchase of Greenland."
* **Trump receives Venezuelan leader 's Nobel Prize, though honor non-transferable:** Trump welcomed 2025 Nobel Peace Prize winner Maria Corina Machado to the White House Thursday where the Venezuelan opposition leader presented him with her medal, though the Nobel committee issued a statement noting that while the medal may be freely given, the actual honor is not transferable, and the Norwegian government has no role in awarding the prize despite Trump's complaints to the prime minister.

* * *
# **Anti-ICE mob storms Minneapolis church during service**
Dozens of agitators burst into the Cities Church sanctuary roughly halfway through Sunday's service believing one of the pastors is the acting director of ICE's St. Paul field office, **[with attendees telling Fox News Digital that agitators followed them into the parking lot as they tried to leave,](https://substack.com/redirect/21ae1311-2c4a-413b-856d-ee37ac945693?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with some surrounding vehicles and trying to block them from departing.
## Watch:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/f47b4186-282b-4f37-9bac-694a01e7cbb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Trump administration launches full DOJ investigation:** Attorney General Pam Bondi said in a statement "I just spoke to the Pastor in Minnesota whose church was targeted. Attacks against law enforcement and the intimidation of Christians are being met with the full force of federal law," adding "If state leaders refuse to act responsibly to prevent lawlessness, this Department of Justice will remain mobilized to prosecute federal crimes," while White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt declared "President Trump will not tolerate the intimidation and harassment of Christians in their sacred places of worship."
* **Assistant AG for Civil Rights vows to deliver justice:** Harmeet Dhillon, the assistant attorney general for civil rights, said she and her team are "hard at work" investigating the incident and "will not rest until we are able to deliver justice," as top-level members of Trump's administration vowed an investigation after the unrest Sunday.
* **Mayor Frey defends protesters, rejects Trump 's characterization:** Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has defended anti-ICE protesters, appearing on CBS' "Face the Nation" Sunday to reject the Trump administration's characterization of the immigration crackdown, arguing "This is not about safety. What this is about is coming into our city by the thousands and terrorizing people simply because they're Latino or Somali," claiming "People in Minneapolis are speaking up. They're speaking up peacefully. They're standing up for their neighbors."

* * *
# **Protester allegedly steals conservative journalist 's $1,000 camera, drags him by car**
Conservative reporter Nick Sortor had **[his pricey camera snatched from his hand](https://substack.com/redirect/b823db29-1ac7-4b49-91ce-dc90740aa8cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while driving in Minneapolis by a woman who was part of a group that had been following him and his crew around the Twin Cities all day, according to Sotor, prompting him to leap out of his car and chase the suspect down the street.
## Watch:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/7e89bff0-51c9-401e-b25e-aec40fbca36a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Sortor dragged down street after hand trapped in door handle:** During the confrontation, Sortor's "hand got trapped in their door handle" and he was "dragged down the street" as the erratic driver swerved onto the icy sidewalk to escape, with footage showing Sortor sliding alongside the moving car before he eventually got his hand loose, though it's unclear how or when he freed himself.
* **Group confronts Sortor, FBI says "We're on it":** After the theft, roughly eight community members circled around Sortor and his crew demanding they leave, with one agitator snapping "Listen here, motherf-ker, you better get the f-k outta here right now!" while Sortor claimed a "group of Somali thugs" followed his car and called on DHS to "raid this place," with the FBI Rapid Response reposting Sortor's X post with the caption "We're on it."

* * *
# **1,500 troops given prepare-to-deploy orders for Minnesota**
The Trump administration is **[preparing 1,500 troops for possible deployment to Democrat-controlled Minnesota](https://substack.com/redirect/bee46c32-93da-481e-87ab-0dcf57938354?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as leftists protest immigration operations, with "the U.S. Army's 11th Airborne Division, which is based in Alaska and specializes in cold-weather and mountain warfare, has given prepare to deploy orders," A senior U.S. official told Fox News.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bee46c32-93da-481e-87ab-0dcf57938354?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump threatens Insurrection Act over attacks on ICE agents:** President Trump threatened to use the Insurrection Act if the Democrat state continues allowing leftists to attack ICE agents, writing "If the corrupt politicians of Minnesota don't obey the law and stop the professional agitators and insurrectionists from attacking the Patriots of I.C.E., who are only trying to do their job, I will institute the INSURRECTION ACT," while Republican lawmakers urged Trump to arrest Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz after he threatened to use the state's National Guard against immigration operations.
* **Protests surge after ICE shooting, fraud scandal deepens:** Leftists mobilized to protest after an ICE agent shot and killed Renee Good when she allegedly weaponized her car as agents were being blocked from performing their work in Minneapolis, bringing more tension to situations involving ICE officers in Minnesota where leaders are being criticized regarding alleged rampant fraud linked to the state's Somali community, with Trump's deputies sending more agents to shield the federal investigation into Democrats' "fraud-and-migration political machine."

* * *

name: Team NPPEF
subject: Celebrating the life of Dr. King ✊🏾
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
Fellow Activist,

Today, the New PA Project Education Fund is commemorating the life and work of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Dr. King dedicated his life to being a leader in the civil rights movement until his life was taken from us too soon. He showed the world that community organizing can lead to critical changes in our democracy that lift up the most vulnerable and protect basic human rights and freedoms.

While we once again find our democracy in peril, we are dedicated to honoring Dr. King’s legacy and stay firm in our commitment of ensuring full participation in the democratic process through civic education and year-round engagement by centering Black, Indigenous, and other people of color, those who are justice impacted, immigrant communities and the youth - with intention - as they are the least represented and most impacted by decisions our government is making.

In his honor, you can find our teams out on the ground across PA assisting eligible Pennsylvanians in registering to vote at the following events:

Western region:
- Allegheny County - Kelly Strayhorn Theater in Pittsburgh
- Erie County - MLK parade downtown

Capital region:
- York County - Crispus Attucks Community Center
- Dauphin County - Goodwin Baptist Church in Harrisburg

Collar counties region:
- Delaware County - "Reclaiming the Dream" Event at Widener University
- Philadelphia - Global Citizen MLK event

We are honored to commemorate Dr. King and Mrs. Coretta Scott King’s lives and legacy today and every day by actively engaging Pennsylvanians on the ground all across the Commonwealth to ensure full participation in the democratic process.

Thank you for joining us in this fight, and happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day of Action!

In solidarity,

Team NPPEF

Donations to the New PA Project Education Fund supports our staff’s voter and community engagement efforts including; voter registration, civic education, organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round.

The New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is a non-partisan organization led by community leaders across the Commonwealth dedicated to centering underrepresented and underserved communities to embrace their power. NPPEF ensures full participation in the democratic process through civic education and year-round engagement by centering Black, Indigenous, and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth - with intention - as they are the least represented and most impacted by decisions our government is making.

Will you support us with a contribution today?

DONATE NOW >> https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nppef-26/?refcode=EM_C_260119MLK_N_TNPPEF_90DO_8_NG_N_PT

Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
New PA Project Education Fund
PO Box 443
West Chester, PA 19381
United States
The New PA Project Education Fund is a qualified 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization and donations are tax-deductible to the full extent allowed under the law.
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/eTvv1eYlREi8TFpa7DUshg2?unsubscribedata=2ZO8S%2Fx4wD7f3iQQ3Jc%2Fx3cJrrjNTUTYcbJF%2BK%2F65YMIUwHKEJJ7hSnj9Z8tqUtlxLTpLza9dMBTaJASokYXyMf2icd5iL2B6MxvOth6rpexrOKhnehO1wr94qqFOsUu9BXkE%2Bbj%2BVEO3mQcACMy8641yt4cSeGt5Ud00vLu5ASDtt8RLfixw%2BUUNUEZU6B6GE2FVyb7RNTc4Vc%2FPj8bz4Lk4IMNWT6SLIoIz6tN0f0WheUNldxMX%2FfuuQhcB9NbSBtsClyPNm8ttRk6qaaIhpNiJ2JYylyHufNfFLZ3bYrN%2BZPdU4fAoddmMz6x0ebimXLe%2BRBLbXInaKwVEXgIpUSu35jjg%2BccuvR0%2BczM8J6TEGVx8nHjufPGWLaspTp%2FZN8BXGmmOxqGVvElb3rAquBMH1zWURWCfrqBaux90iM%3D .

name: MassDems featuring Brian & Chris Worrell
subject: Dr. King is not a symbol to be celebrated once a year.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Dear Friends,
Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. once warned, “We are not makers of history. We are made by history.” In this moment, history is again pressing upon us asking who we are, what we believe, and whether we will meet the gravity of the times with courage or retreat into comfort.
Dr. King is not a symbol to be celebrated once a year. He is a moral reckoning. His life and sacrifice demand more than remembrance; they demand honesty. He challenged power, disrupted complacency, and spoke truths that made institutions uncomfortable, especially when democracy itself was under threat.
Today, we write as brothers Brian Worrell, a Boston City Councilor, and Christopher Worrell, a State Representative, linked not only by blood, but by a shared responsibility to the people we serve and the values that brought us into public life. Our brotherhood is rooted in community, shaped by struggle, and guided by a belief that justice is not abstract, it is lived, fought for, and defended every day.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/1_2.png]
At the federal level, we are witnessing an alarming unraveling. Democratic norms are being eroded. Voting rights are under assault. Truth is routinely distorted. The dignity of immigrants, working families, Black communities, and marginalized people is treated as expendable. Dr. King cautioned us about moments like this when he said, “The time is always right to do what is right.” He did not say it would be easy. He did not say it would be popular. He said it would be necessary.
Dr. King also reminded us that “A nation that continues year after year to spend more money on military defense than on programs of social uplift is approaching spiritual death.” As federal priorities drift further from moral clarity, states like Massachusetts are no longer insulated observers we are moral actors. Silence, neutrality, or delay is itself a choice.
Our communities are watching. Young people are watching. History is watching.
Dr. King understood that the greatest danger was not loud hatred alone, but quiet indifference. He warned that “In the end, we will remember not the words of our enemies, but the silence of our friends.” That warning echoes loudly today.
We honor Dr. King not by invoking his name, but by refusing to betray his legacy. His work was unfinished because justice is unfinished. Democracy is unfinished.
And the struggle for dignity—for all people—remains unfinished.
In Service,
Brian Worrell
Boston City Councilor
Christopher Worrell
State Representative, 5th Suffolk District

name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
It’s up to everyday Americans to bend that arc of the moral universe towards justice.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123600851/595979175/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjA5OTY3LTNmZjUtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMjI0ODJkMjc5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9KpYICOpO6I32a7LWxduW9gFhLobnq6DbVPsyO18gqg=&emci=b5262605-16f3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=5db09967-3ff5-f011-8d4c-0022482d279b&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Today, we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and reflect on his legacy of principled and powerful resistance. I am also reflecting today on Dr. King’s ability to speak plainly about injustice and fearlessly confront the forces that stood in the way of freedom while never losing his belief in the American people’s ability to walk together toward the Promised Land. Our current moment demands such clarity and such fearlessness. The forces reshaping our country are testing not only our institutions, but our collective capacity to defend them. And they require from all of us a clarity of purpose that is equal to the rising stakes. We are seeing a sustained effort to abolish the Voting Rights Act — the crown jewel of the Civil Rights Movement and Dr. King’s legacy. We are witnessing the weaponization of the Department of Justice by the president to silence his critics and intimidate voters. We’re experiencing unprecedented mid-decade gerrymandering attempts designed to disproportionately disenfranchise Black and Brown voters as the president desperately clings to power like an insecure dictator. But as dark as things may seem, we must remember that every generation has been similarly tested. Each time, Americans faced a critical question: Sit back and hope help comes along, or stand together and fight for what we believe in? I know the scale of what we’re up against can feel overwhelming. It’s only human to hope for someone else to step in. To pray for a miracle or a savior. To look to the horizon, hoping to see the cavalry coming to the rescue. But here’s the hard truth: There are no reinforcements on the way. No “miraculous rescue” is coming. **We are the cavalry. ** Dr. King understood this acutely. **“Human progress is neither automatic nor inevitable,”** he once said. “Even a casual look at history reveals that no social advance rolls in on the wheels of inevitability. Every step toward the goal of justice requires sacrifice, suffering, and struggle; the tireless exertions and passionate concern of dedicated individuals.” Dedicated individuals like you have always carried this work forward. Democracy does not defend itself. From the fights for due process to the struggle for access to the ballot, it’s been everyday Americans who have bent that arc of the moral universe towards justice. We owe a profound debt to the generations who sacrificed so much to secure these rights, and I hope you’ll join me in spending some time reflecting on that sacrifice today. And tomorrow, we’ll continue to honor them by picking up the torch they passed us — by doing the work we must do and carrying forward with the march they never finished. **Because we owe a similar debt to generations yet to come. We can only truly honor our past by ensuring those who come after us will inherit an America truer to its promise.** I hope you’ll continue to [**support NDRC’s work,**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123600857/595979188/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGMLKDay_email_20260119-NDRC-Tier1-AGMLKDay_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260119-NDRC-Tier1-AGMLKDay_&utm_campaign=AGMLKDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjA5OTY3LTNmZjUtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMjI0ODJkMjc5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9KpYICOpO6I32a7LWxduW9gFhLobnq6DbVPsyO18gqg=) and to fight for a fairer, more truly representative America. Thank you, Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123600859/595979228/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGMLKDay_email_20260119-NDRC-Tier1-AGMLKDay_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260119-NDRC-Tier1-AGMLKDay_&utm_campaign=AGMLKDay&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjA5OTY3LTNmZjUtZjAxMS04ZDRjLTAwMjI0ODJkMjc5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9KpYICOpO6I32a7LWxduW9gFhLobnq6DbVPsyO18gqg=)

| | | | | |
# Kevin’s Proven Record Protecting Families During COVID Overreach
---
| | Dear Friend and Patriot,
I've always believed in straight talk and facts - especially on government overreach that hit Michigan families hard.
When COVID mandates threatened schools as superintendent for Detroit's Catholic schools, here's what I did to protect parental choice and in-person learning:
_✓_ In September 2020, knowing legal challenges were actively being prepared that I also consulted on, I encouraged temporary compliance to prevent immediate shutdowns by the government that would have closed many schools and disrupted tens of thousands of students.
_✓_ In October 2020, my colleagues in the Diocese of Lansing filed the anticipated lawsuit against mask requirements that would have ramifications for all children across the state. [Click here to view the story](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=76e9d2421a&e=4b10910eed).
_✓_ In November 2020, as Vice Chair of the Michigan Association of Non-public Schools, I led efforts to organize federal litigation - [along with additional support from President Trump and his Administration](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=96c1457d37&e=4b10910eed) \- that stopped Governor Whitmer's high school shutdown, keeping tens of thousands of students learning in person from across the state and that forced the government to back down. [Click here to view the story](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=f74add6260&e=4b10910eed).

This was effective leadership through real teamwork, coordination, and the courts - delivering results for families when it mattered most.
I wrote about this experience and the lasting threat of overreach in a November opinion piece published in The Midwesterner about two months ago. [You can read it by clicking here](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=ab74ff25e9&e=4b10910eed).[](https://kevinformi.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=39782b0ca6b504d2faad7a196&id=1860a0c67c&e=4b10910eed)
As your next Attorney General, I'll bring that same determination: defending constitutional rights, protecting student and parental rights, and standing strong for public safety and family freedoms.
---
| | **Thank you for your continued support. Honored to earn it with proven results.**
Kevin Kijewski
Republican Candidate for Michigan Attorney General
---

name: Trisha
subject: MLK Day under the 2nd Trump admin: From despair to progress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Solidarity forever. Dr. King once said, “The labor movement was the principal force that transformed misery and despair into hope and progress.” Those words still ring true today.
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001XvBc3bsHgRWXLyJfHoB0-w%3D&ch=005337f2-e4bf-11f0-bd14-0242b661c010&ca=16c3d9c1-9c3e-4e51-931e-28defc29bc2b) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.
---
---
| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kHlc2nu8DDavzd4djEv6NYHdxteO5bcPxOESqws5hkv--kkAY0Mga3A4mmroaI0tjjyfyxG4g8HaWDQ-rKszJ4KbgHzNo5t2xjk7EfsOZOlOhqlvcD9Y743YqE8AH744yra_lhi1V-0MKT0P7euqtxykvplyWr4eSgokvwWgidxbyUf4Z_SoAw-RjWUfcCnaFSDu5nHxvuNDrxNQo5EEOihgwtryAZsy&c=rk2pWjIz29Ypi4UGSswB5F0Bo8iTqQfsTVG-pW0I7c-nTDGT7Ya6pg==&ch=qupOaxcFayTjvy9sPIRs4wuTLJlIVXy6L1mS8Y3YzHLgLmT583Sdrw==)
---
| Peter,
Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
It is a holiday honoring one of the most important figures in American history. A civil rights leader who helped bend the moral arc of this country toward justice for people oppressed simply because of the color of their skin.
But Dr. King's legacy does not stop there.
He was a fierce champion for working people and labor unions. He understood that racial justice and economic justice are inseparable. That dignity on the job is a civil right. That democracy does not mean much if working families are crushed while the wealthy and powerful take everything.That's why the historic march on Washington where he delivered his iconic "I have a Dream" speech was for Jobs and Freedom.
Dr. King once said, "The labor movement was the principal force that transformed misery and despair into hope and progress." Those words still ring true today.
And they feel painfully prescient in this moment.
Right now, we are living under an administration that is unleashing federal force with no oversight through masked, unidentified agents detaining people, shooting people, and surveilling us online. A president who openly attacks minority communities and even threatens to strip people of their citizenship. A president who is no friend to labor, attacking collective bargaining rights and gutting agencies like OSHA and the National Labor Relations Board, whose sole mission is to protect workers from abuse by the wealthy and the elite.
It can feel dark. Overwhelming. Even hopeless at times.
But on a day like today, Dr. King's legacy shines a light forward.
He reminds us that progress has never been handed down from the top. It has always been won by ordinary people standing together. Workers organizing. Communities refusing to be silent. People choosing courage over fear.
As Dr. King said, "We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope."
That hope is what fuels this campaign. It is why I am running. When I worked with America's unions at the national AFL-CIO, I fought tirelessly for working families. That fight is a relentless force behind our campaign.
My opponent Lauren Boebert has been enabling Trump's racist and anti-labor agenda. And that's why she is more vulnerable than she has ever been. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024 when I faced Boebert, our campaign outperformed VP Kamala Harris by nearly seven points. Now our campaign just outraised her by over a million dollars.
[If you are able, I am asking you to make a contribution of $25, $15 or whatever is meaningful to you today in honor of Dr. King's legacy. Your support helps us fight for workers, protect our communities, and keep pushing this country toward justice, dignity, and equality for all.](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001kHlc2nu8DDavzd4djEv6NYHdxteO5bcPxOESqws5hkv--kkAY0Mga3A4mmroaI0t60_8V4p3hpm608tSKHDzO9SYHWlBQCDvFd3VT1wtXr1VTyb3zb-S0M404aWf2AVHOtU1oyE_VMrC2Bpm-YGxD-aNEFpHqKZ4P03Ua4SElu5mywrp4zthZeJE9wXg3-bpA7RjkYMyCCZ-XVYkwHiL_wvTzDyS6mCPiCKiXqYzc0w=&c=rk2pWjIz29Ypi4UGSswB5F0Bo8iTqQfsTVG-pW0I7c-nTDGT7Ya6pg==&ch=qupOaxcFayTjvy9sPIRs4wuTLJlIVXy6L1mS8Y3YzHLgLmT583Sdrw==)

Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | Peter, Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day. It is a holiday honoring one of the most important figures in American history. A civil rights leader who helped bend the moral arc of this country toward justice for people oppressed simply because of the color of their skin. But Dr. King's legacy does not stop there. He was a fierce champion for working people and labor unions. He understood that racial justice and economic justice are inseparable. That dignity on the job is a civil right. That democracy does not mean much if working families are crushed while the wealthy and powerful take everything.That's why the historic march on Washington where he delivered his iconic "I have a Dream" speech was for Jobs and Freedom. Dr. King once said, "The labor movement was the principal force that transformed misery and despair into hope and progress." Those words still ring true today. And they feel painfully prescient in this moment. Right now, we are living under an administration that is unleashing federal force with no oversight through masked, unidentified agents detaining people, shooting people, and surveilling us online. A president who openly attacks minority communities and even threatens to strip people of their citizenship. A president who is no friend to labor, attacking collective bargaining rights and gutting agencies like OSHA and the National Labor Relations Board, whose sole mission is to protect workers from abuse by the wealthy and the elite. It can feel dark. Overwhelming. Even hopeless at times. But on a day like today, Dr. King's legacy shines a light forward. He reminds us that progress has never been handed down from the top. It has always been won by ordinary people standing together. Workers organizing. Communities refusing to be silent. People choosing courage over fear. * * *
_**by Ellie Leonard, BAM Contributing Editor**_
_**I wrote this piece last fall in the wake of Trey Reed, found hanging from a tree at Delta State University in Mississippi. The story quietly went away, and we never found out if his death was investigated thoroughly or if the family had closure.**_
_**Trey 's story, and every story targeting people for race, ethnicity, culture, religion, and country of origin, before and since, is something we are supposed to learn from. All of us know Winston Churchill's famous quote, "Those that fail to learn from history are doomed to repeat it," but I often wonder what people learned from a history that may have benefitted them in the long run. Is it so much that we didn't learn, or is it that we didn't condemn it in the first place? Did white supremacists not learn? Did xenophobes and supporters of ICE on the streets of Minneapolis, ripping people from their cars, homes, and jobs, not learn? Or is it that they learned too much about their own power and influence? I don't have the answer.**_
_**But for me, I knew that my power to be an ally, an activist, and a warrior for the people around me who continue to be targeted by Donald Trump 's white nationalist administration meant I needed to educate myself about the things I never learned in school. I needed to know about colonialism. I needed to understand the Native American boarding school system. I needed to learn about what really happened at Selma, and why, and the aftermath. It's thousands of pages of history missed from my childhood education.**_
_**But I have time.**_
_**The following piece of history is an integral part of the Civil Rights Movement, and something most of us, in the North, at least, never learned in school. I hope it brings us one step closer to actual civil rights, human rights, and equality for all people.**_
On September 15, 2025, Delta State University student Demartravion "Trey" Reed was discovered hanging from a tree near the pickleball courts on campus. While we don't know the circumstances that led up to Trey's death, we certainly shouldn't be satisfied with "case closed" after decades of racial injustice that included over 4,000 lynchings of black men, women, and children, but also Mexicans, Italians, Chinese, and Native Americans.
The Panicked, Unpaid Writer is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
There is very little public information about Trey's life prior to his death, other than he graduated from Grenada High School in Grenada, Mississippi, in 2023 with 235 of his classmates. He posted a picture of himself on his first day of college on August 20th, along with several quotes indicating relationship struggles.
"Lord protect the path I'm on & don't let me quit for nothin." \- Trey Reed
"P R A Y, W A I T, & T R U S T." \- Trey Reed
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Grenada High School
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.uVRYYjUAuJlQ-q73caLs73AN84Wy78Y-BthVqjRJtu4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=184978081)
* * *
At this point, we don't know what happened to Trey, but history doesn't allow for complacency or obedience, not by the groups that were targeted, and (we hope) not by the groups who targeted them. We need to be asking questions about how this young man ended up hanging from a tree. In the meantime, the rest of us need to educate ourselves about the history of racial violence in the United States and the things we were never taught in school.
_So today we 'll learn about the Tulsa Race Massacre, considered one of the worst incidents of racial violence in American history._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Tulsa Race Riots - Wikipedia
The Tulsa Race Massacre, or "race riot" took place between May 31st and June 1st, 1921, in the Greenwood District in Tulsa, Oklahoma, when white supremacist residents--some of whom worked for the police departments and city government--attacked black residents and destroyed their homes and businesses. When all was said and done, 36 people were dead, 800 were injured, 6000 black residents were displaced, and 35 square blocks were completely gone. A follow-up investigation in 2001 by the Tulsa Reparations Coalition estimated there could have been as many as 300 killed, due to hurried, undocumented burials.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=184978081&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0OTc4MDgxLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzQzNzAsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNjM3MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lSbmZhvSgXpC5YoiUElwCa8KoJ-pvESY45oDGabC44Q)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
James Jones, also known as "Diamond Dick" Rowland, seated third from left, front row. Courtesy of Booker T. Washington High School
Like many tragic events in the history of racial injustice, the Tulsa Race Massacre began with the false accusation of a black man assaulting a white woman, in this case, 19-year-old Dick Rowland, a shoe shiner at the Main Street Shine Parlor, and 21-year-old elevator operator Sarah Page. When Rowland tripped entering the Drexel Building elevator to go up to use the segregated bathroom, a white store clerk reported the "attack" to authorities, crying rape. At the time, Tulsa was a Jim Crow city, and it was illegal for black residents to use white bathroom facilities. When Rowland tripped, he reached out and grabbed Page's arm to steady himself. She screamed, and Rowland found himself cuffed and being led away by police officers.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The Tulsa Tribune
The Tulsa Tribune would later report that Rowland had torn Page's clothes, and allegedly included the subtext "To Lynch Negro Tonight." Rowland, however, was well-known by local officials, lawyers, and businessmen, from his work shining shoes, and most didn't bat an eye.
> #### "Why, I know that boy, and have known him a good while. That's not in him." \- unknown customer
A mob of angry white men came to the courthouse, and rumors began to spread about a lynching. Sixty of Rowland's supporters showed up, thinking he'd already been killed.
> #### "I saw a car full of negroes driving through the streets with guns; I saw Bill McCullough and told him those negroes would cause trouble; McCullough tried to talk to them, and they got out and stood in single file. W. G. Daggs was killed near Boulder and Sixth street. I was under the impression that a man with authority could have stopped and disarmed them. I saw Chief of Police on south side of courthouse on top step, talking; I did not see any officer except the Chief; I walked in the court house and met McCullough in about 15 feet of his door; I told him these negroes were going to make trouble, and he said he had told them to go home; he went out and told the Whites to go home, and one said: "they said you told them to come up here." McCullough said "I did not" and a negro said you did tell us to come." \- witness
Sheriff McCullough promised to keep Rowland safe in jail from the now nearly 1000-person mob, and the black men turned to leave. He positioned six armed officers around the courthouse and shut down all the elevators, telling them to "shoot any intruders in sight." But he couldn't appease the angry mob outside, all white, and was "hooted down." The crowd swelled to 2000. It wasn't until an old white man approached O.B. Mann, a black World War I veteran, and tried to take his gun, that "all hell broke loose." In all, 12 people were killed, 10 white and 2 black. News traveled quickly, and more white men showed up, chasing the black protestors back to Greenwood, where the mob started fires and even (allegedly) dropped firebombs from private airplanes owned by local residents.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Tulsa Race Riots - Wikipedia
In the middle of the chaos, members of the mob were deputized by a police officer and instructed to "get a gun and get a nigger." Tulsa's co-founder and KKK member, W. Tate Brad,y participated as a night watchman. He'd previously led the Tulsa Outrage, which tarred and feathered members of the Industrial Workers of the World in World War I.
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=184978081&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg0OTc4MDgxLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzQzNzAsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNjM3MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lSbmZhvSgXpC5YoiUElwCa8KoJ-pvESY45oDGabC44Q&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
By the next morning, men lay dead, strewn about the streets, and the stores left standing had been looted. The Oklahoma National Guard imposed martial law, and the massacre came to an end. $2.25 million in real estate and personal property was gone, equivalent to nearly $41 million in 2025, and 10,000 black men, women, and children were homeless. In all, 1256 houses burned, destroying "Black Wall Street," one of the wealthiest black communities in the United States. Some chose to leave, others to rebuild, but the city didn't compensate them for their loss, and the story disappeared from the record books.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Aftermath of the Tulsa Race Riots - Wikipedia
It wouldn't be until 2001 that the Oklahoma Commission to Study the Tulsa Race Riot of 1921 would publish its final report and recommend reparations to the descendants of the survivors. There is no written statement from Sarah Page, and she never pressed charges against Dick Rowland, whose case was dropped
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The Morning Tulsa Daily World listed the leader of the black rioters, Will Robinson, as "a dope peddler and all-around bad negro," and said the rest of them were "former servicemen who had an exaggerated idea of their importance... They did not belong here, had no regular employment, and were simply a floating element with seemingly no ambition in life but to foment trouble."
Starting in 2002, Oklahoma was required to teach students about the massacre, and in 2020 officially developed a curriculum based on the history. However, Project 2025 is going after any curriculum that covers Critical Race Theory (CRT), or the concept that racism is "deeply woven into the fabric of American institutions and policies," and this likely will reverse any previous requirements to study Tulsa's history of segregation and racism.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Facebook
These were the things _I_ , at least, was not taught in 13 years of public school, and 4 years of higher education. But these are the stories that tell us to keep asking questions when a 21-year-old black man is found hanging from a tree in the State of Mississippi, which, according to the NAACP, held the record for the highest number of lynchings in any state from 1882 to 1968. It's why we shouldn't take "no" for an answer, why we shouldn't believe in a "closed case" that took less than a day, and why we should demand that law enforcement be held accountable.
But sadly, the _best-case_ scenario is that Trey was sad and didn't ask for help. Is that something I hope for? Feels odd. But maybe so. Because so much of our life, our beliefs, our laws, social mores, dynamics, our love-thy-neighbors, has gone full-throttle in reverse since at least January 20th of this 2025.
And I hope to God this isn't another sign of things to come.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1df4b-7be4-444d-bec6-b2b04840de29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

Why Trey Reed's death forces America to reckon with its unfinished civil rights history | | and | Jan 19 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- by Ellie Leonard, BAM Contributing Editor I wrote this piece last fall in the wake of Trey Reed, found hanging from a tree at Delta State University in Mississippi. The story quietly went away, and we never found out if his death was investigated thoroughly or if the family had closure. Trey 's story, and every story targeting people for race, ethnicity, culture, religion, and country of origin, before and since, is something we are supposed to learn from. All of us know Winston Churchill's famous quote, "Those that fail to learn from history are doomed to repeat it," but I often wonder what people learned from a history that may have benefitted them in the long run. Is it so much that we didn't learn, or is it that we didn't condemn it in the first place? Did white supremacists not learn? Did xenophobes and supporters of ICE on the streets of Minneapolis, ripping people from their cars, homes, and jobs, not learn? Or is it that they learned too much about their own power and influence? I don't have the answer. But for me, I knew that my power to be an ally, an activist, and a warrior for the people around me who continue to be targeted by Donald Trump 's white nationalist administration meant I needed to educate myself about the things I never learned in school. I needed to know about colonialism. I needed to understand the Native American boarding school system. I needed to learn about what really happened at Selma, and why, and the aftermath. It's thousands of pages of history missed from my childhood education. But I have time. The following piece of history is an integral part of the Civil Rights Movement, and something most of us, in the North, at least, never learned in school. I hope it brings us one step closer to actual civil rights, human rights, and equality for all people. On September 15, 2025, Delta State University student Demartravion "Trey" Reed was discovered hanging from a tree near the pickleball courts on campus. While we don't know the circumstances that led up to Trey's death, we certainly shouldn't be satisfied with "case closed" after decades of racial injustice that included over 4,000 lynchings of black men, women, and children, but also Mexicans, Italians, Chinese, and Native Americans. The Panicked, Unpaid Writer is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. There is very little public information about Trey's life prior to his death, other than he graduated from Grenada High School in Grenada, Mississippi, in 2023 with 235 of his classmates. He posted a picture of himself on his first day of college on August 20th, along with several quotes indicating relationship struggles. "Lord protect the path I'm on & don't let me quit for nothin." \- Trey Reed "P R A Y, W A I T, & T R U S T." \- Trey Reed | | ---|---|--- Grenada High School At this point, we don't know what happened to Trey, but history doesn't allow for complacency or obedience, not by the groups that were targeted, and (we hope) not by the groups who targeted them. We need to be asking questions about how this young man ended up hanging from a tree. In the meantime, the rest of us need to educate ourselves about the history of racial violence in the United States and the things we were never taught in school. So today we 'll learn about the Tulsa Race Massacre, considered one of the worst incidents of racial violence in American history. | | ---|---|--- Tulsa Race Riots - Wikipedia The Tulsa Race Massacre, or "race riot" took place between May 31st and June 1st, 1921, in the Greenwood District in Tulsa, Oklahoma, when white supremacist residents--some of whom worked for the police departments and city government--attacked black residents and destroyed their homes and businesses. When all was said and done, 36 people were dead, 800 were injured, 6000 black residents were displaced, and 35 square blocks were completely gone. A follow-up investigation in 2001 by the Tulsa Reparations Coalition estimated there could have been as many as 300 killed, due to hurried, undocumented burials. | | ---|---|--- James Jones, also known as "Diamond Dick" Rowland, seated third from left, front row. Courtesy of Booker T. Washington High School Like many tragic events in the history of racial injustice, the Tulsa Race Massacre began with the false accusation of a black man assaulting a white woman, in this case, 19-year-old Dick Rowland, a shoe shiner at the Main Street Shine Parlor, and 21-year-old elevator operator Sarah Page. When Rowland tripped entering the Drexel Building elevator to go up to use the segregated bathroom, a white store clerk reported the "attack" to authorities, crying rape. At the time, Tulsa was a Jim Crow city, and it was illegal for black residents to use white bathroom facilities. When Rowland tripped, he reached out and grabbed Page's arm to steady himself. She screamed, and Rowland found himself cuffed and being led away by police officers. | | ---|---|--- The Tulsa Tribune The Tulsa Tribune would later report that Rowland had torn Page's clothes, and allegedly included the subtext "To Lynch Negro Tonight." Rowland, however, was well-known by local officials, lawyers, and businessmen, from his work shining shoes, and most didn't bat an eye. #### "Why, I know that boy, and have known him a good while. That's not in him." \- unknown customer A mob of angry white men came to the courthouse, and rumors began to spread about a lynching. W. G. Daggs was killed near Boulder and Sixth street. I was under the impression that a man with authority could have stopped and disarmed them. I saw Chief of Police on south side of courthouse on top step, talking; I did not see any officer except the Chief; I walked in the court house and met McCullough in about 15 feet of his door; I told him these negroes were going to make trouble, and he said he had told them to go home; he went out and told the Whites to go home, and one said: "they said you told them to come up here." McCullough said "I did not" and a negro said you did tell us to come." \- witness Sheriff McCullough promised to keep Rowland safe in jail from the now nearly 1000-person mob, and the black men turned to leave. He positioned six armed officers around the courthouse and shut down all the elevators, telling them to "shoot any intruders in sight." He'd previously led the Tulsa Outrage, which tarred and feathered members of the Industrial Workers of the World in World War I. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Commemorating Dr. Martin Luther King Jr’s Legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Today, we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s life and leadership. Dr. King changed the composition of our democracy for the better and pushed this country to live up to its promise. His work pushed the Civil Rights movement forward and changed the course of our history. Each year, this day reminds us of how far we’ve come, and how far we still have to go to achieve true equality in America. So today isn’t just about remembering the past. It’s about continuing Dr. King’s fight for justice and equal opportunity. As history and Dr. King’s legacy tell us, only light can drive out darkness. Today, I encourage each of us to honor his legacy not just with words, but with action and volunteerism. \- Kirsten Gillibrand
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

Click here to [unsubscribe](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/46LjluwB30OIUfgIWihL4Q2?unsubscribedata=PuY1bF%2BR%2B%2BBq9phkyEEzgjQ6B6bGu5wrFsuiHqLk6q8ISOVwGsjWnQyfLn87ceRszwZQpqGiVOQ52GpptKLaAxzMJDpvqRPHyrvY3%2B98mHq%2FZ%2BD9dQ2hOavG%2BX%2FMxry9frGXBxvKAeYC2AIp%2FN%2F15bsFo8jPDhx4J1DWrdk%2FL0UMXgJLO3K5oxcb2gbDxx6tb7d7j%2FJTFAfhvolZc%2B%2FO83WQ8Zx3PChy6tgmcfjxnfHgIPjEXngkd4PQxHqomLheWqMjNpRyWjgiZ%2FYoopzSlRjk0wte4OhjfyiCrH5tk25ubFM7m6H4N3TvzxXU9s%2BHuCI7C8R44uq1w%2FDs2OH12ItSUPkYN%2BpnLhN8qGxqkT6O07dM0vVcLPgS4YhE3Fyd95UdRB%2FGl2RgcRl7KMtcleKhzHHBUcLuvFUJbRFwZ8w%3D). body:
Dear Peter,
It's Mark from House GOP. Honestly, these are always the hardest emails to write.
But at this point, we're seriously starting to get worried.
[Either you quit on us, or you’re simply ignoring these requests. name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: And we march on.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=3431%2E52803%2E_uXSYn)
| | When I think about what Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day means to me and my family, I think about my great-grandma, Maebel James. She was born in the segregated South in 1896, just 33 years after the Emancipation Proclamation. She lost her husband to racist violence. When the perpetrators of that crime threatened the lives of her children, she uprooted her family and fled the South to seek refuge here in Maryland. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2571?t=6&akid=3431%2E52803%2E_uXSYn) Her life was not an easy one. Still, Maebel was never bitter, always there to be the anchor of her family, the living embodiment of empathy, resilience, and dignity. Maebel witnessed our country change and transform — sometimes for the better, other times not — and she faced it all with grace, empathy, and love. Especially now, it’s worth remembering that the path to civil rights wasn’t easy or straightforward. While we’ve made tremendous progress in the past half century, our fight for justice continues. Healthcare and housing inequality, criminal injustice, and bigotry persist. We haven’t yet achieved the dream of equality and love that Dr. King so eloquently described at the Lincoln Memorial, 100 years after the Emancipation Proclamation. But we shouldn’t forget that Dr. King never stopped believing in the promise of America. He believed, as did my great-grandmother, that in spite of our flaws, that we are a nation determined to be better tomorrow than we were yesterday. They were right. My family’s story is proof of that — from the segregated Deep South to the United States Senate. So we march on, Peter, even when the path is steep and uncertain. As my great-grandmother always told me, "_Go farther and do better."_ Together,
Angela Alsobrooks [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2571?t=7&akid=3431%2E52803%2E_uXSYn)

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: Charlie wrote this for the soul
body:
| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e8756536/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvSlRHdGthSHl1ag/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjAxMTk/LbmNsNnZ3U1lNQQ/qP2hpZHM9bmNON29JWEdtY2V4R3Fscw/gaW4-Gw/s3va22e639a)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)
---
| | Patriot,
---
| | Charlie would have wanted you to have this.

Before his September 10th assassination, Charlie had just put the finishing touches on a very important book. His final book.

The topic is one that Charlie found to be crucial for every human to understand and practice.

This isn’t a book about public policy or political discourse, nor is it simply about passing on values of patriotism to young Americans.

**Instead, this book is about something far deeper and more personal — the importance of honoring the Sabbath and resting in God’s grace.**

Despite his hectic schedule and full life, Charlie was committed to setting aside an entire day each week to rest, to pray, and to give undivided attention to God, Erika, and his children.

By honoring the Sabbath, Charlie lived out a powerful message: _**“God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold.”**_

His book, _**Stop, in the Name of God**_ is the fruit of that conviction.
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)
---
When you read it, you will hear Charlie’s voice inviting you to reclaim something our fast-paced culture has essentially erased: a time for rest, a time devoted to God, and time with family.

Charlie’s hope was for each of us to rediscover the Sabbath as he did: not as a burden to bear, but as a gift to receive...

A sacred routine meant to draw us nearer to God, to steady our hearts with His peace, and renew our strength for the battles to which He calls us.

I believe you will find this book inspiring.

Charlie wanted everyone to have this book because its message meant so much in his own life.

**[To claim your copy of this life-changing book, simply make a generous gift in any amount to Turning Point USA today.](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie’s Final Book** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)
---
In the months since Charlie’s assassination, there has been a massive surge of interest in Turning Point USA’s message among young Americans...

...and it’s not just the conservative message as a whole — _**it’s the message of FAITH!**_

In recent months, I have heard countless stories of people who have answered Charlie’s call to attend a Bible study, start a family, and pray to God.

Charlie devoted so much of his time urging people to build strong families, to go to church, and to stand up for America. _And it’s happening._

After years of witnessing declining church attendance, we are now seeing Americans filling the pews in huge numbers across the country.

What we’re witnessing is not just growth —**it’s revival.**

At Turning Point USA, we are dedicated and honored to continue strengthening the faith of Americans, young and old.

We are building a coalition of truth-preaching churches that stand firm against cultural compromise, defend biblical values, and strengthen families.

We are training pastors, uniting congregations, and, of course, reaching the next generation on campuses across the country with this same message.

You might feel disheartened by the evil in our culture and by the unthinkable assassination of Charlie.

**But America’s faithful are waking up. The youth are waking up. America is waking up.**

All across this nation, pastors are linking arms, young believers are finding their voice, and families are saying, _“We’re not backing down.”_

This is what Charlie prayed for.

I see this book as one of Charlie’s most enduring gifts to the world, and I know you will too.

None of us knew how brief his time on earth would be. But the truths written in this book are timeless. They will outlive us all, and most importantly, **_so will the legacy of Charlie’s faith._**

Charlie lived life at a relentless pace, but he also understood the sacred value of stillness. Through it all, his gaze was fixed on eternity with God.

**[To claim your copy of Charlie’s final book, simply make a gift, in any amount, to support Turning Point USA today.](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie’s Final Book** ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.-Gw/s1jed0f28ab)
---
I’ll personally ensure your copy is sent to you as soon as possible.

clean_body: | | | | | Patriot, | | Charlie would have wanted you to have this. Before his September 10th assassination, Charlie had just put the finishing touches on a very important book. His final book. The topic is one that Charlie found to be crucial for every human to understand and practice. This isn’t a book about public policy or political discourse, nor is it simply about passing on values of patriotism to young Americans. Instead, this book is about something far deeper and more personal — the importance of honoring the Sabbath and resting in God’s grace. Despite his hectic schedule and full life, Charlie was committed to setting aside an entire day each week to rest, to pray, and to give undivided attention to God, Erika, and his children. By honoring the Sabbath, Charlie lived out a powerful message: “God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold.” His book, Stop, in the Name of God is the fruit of that conviction. When you read it, you will hear Charlie’s voice inviting you to reclaim something our fast-paced culture has essentially erased: a time for rest, a time devoted to God, and time with family. Charlie’s hope was for each of us to rediscover the Sabbath as he did: not as a burden to bear, but as a gift to receive... A sacred routine meant to draw us nearer to God, to steady our hearts with His peace, and renew our strength for the battles to which He calls us. I believe you will find this book inspiring. Charlie wanted everyone to have this book because its message meant so much in his own life. In the months since Charlie’s assassination, there has been a massive surge of interest in Turning Point USA’s message among young Americans... ...and it’s not just the conservative message as a whole — it’s the message of FAITH! In recent months, I have heard countless stories of people who have answered Charlie’s call to attend a Bible study, start a family, and pray to God. Charlie devoted so much of his time urging people to build strong families, to go to church, and to stand up for America. And it’s happening. After years of witnessing declining church attendance, we are now seeing Americans filling the pews in huge numbers across the country. What we’re witnessing is not just growth —it’s revival. At Turning Point USA, we are dedicated and honored to continue strengthening the faith of Americans, young and old. We are building a coalition of truth-preaching churches that stand firm against cultural compromise, defend biblical values, and strengthen families. We are training pastors, uniting congregations, and, of course, reaching the next generation on campuses across the country with this same message. You might feel disheartened by the evil in our culture and by the unthinkable assassination of Charlie. But America’s faithful are waking up. The youth are waking up. America is waking up. All across this nation, pastors are linking arms, young believers are finding their voice, and families are saying, “We’re not backing down.” This is what Charlie prayed for. I see this book as one of Charlie’s most enduring gifts to the world, and I know you will too. None of us knew how brief his time on earth would be. But the truths written in this book are timeless. They will outlive us all, and most importantly, so will the legacy of Charlie’s faith. Charlie lived life at a relentless pace, but he also understood the sacred value of stillness. Through it all, his gaze was fixed on eternity with God. I’ll personally ensure your copy is sent to you as soon as possible. Thank you in advance for carrying on Charlie’s mission and supporting his legacy. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S. When you read Stop, in the Name of God, you will discover what Charlie found in the Sabbath: a holy rest that steadies the heart, renews the soul, and gives strength for the road ahead. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

subject: What’s Happening in Iran & Venezuela Matters for the Rest of the World

Turmoil in Iran, and U.S. action in Venezuela are shifting the global order

# [What's Happening in Iran & Venezuela Matters for the Rest of the World](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185066152&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDY2MTUyLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzI5MDUsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNDkwNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T9D1heE4MIUJKs80pk8b0B1Yz57Z9ZfaN5qsYDUGK8s)
### Turmoil in Iran, and U.S. action in Venezuela are shifting the global order
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Jan 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185066152&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDY2MTUyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2ODgzMjkwNSwiZXhwIjoxNzcxNDI0OTA1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.LmtYddrYRINpQYY7SlGCJgvldfU9EA0se5mTbtlhWcU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185066152&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDY2MTUyLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzI5MDUsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNDkwNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T9D1heE4MIUJKs80pk8b0B1Yz57Z9ZfaN5qsYDUGK8s&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=185066152&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDY2MTUyLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzI5MDUsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNDkwNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.T9D1heE4MIUJKs80pk8b0B1Yz57Z9ZfaN5qsYDUGK8s)
---
| | [](https://substack..U1Fy-t6pIFLGhLS9il4wxR0b9W2oy3mSTKLEppx74Wk?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/whats-happening-in-iran-and-venezuela?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

We're just a couple weeks into 2026, and already two geopolitical shocks have restructured the global landscape.

First, the United States moved into Venezuela and removed its de facto leader, Nicolas Maduro.

Now, protests across Iran have sparked the most defiant challenge to clerical rule since Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei came to power in 1979.

Together, these two events mark a sharp break from the geopolitical stasis that defined much of the past few decades.
Iran and Venezuela are bound by more than political timing. Both are energy-rich states ruled by deeply unpopular, anti-democratic regimes that have maintained power through repression while steadily impoverishing civilian life. What is now unfolding in both countries reflects the breaking point.
The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..hQU2LzON3Vq9uGDgq6SDgTJnSZD07b-220-71qosuUA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=185066152)
Iran is experiencing its deadliest unrest since the 1979 revolution. Credible reports indicate that [at least 5,000 people have been killed](https://substack.com/redirect/211a4376-2999-4cd0-8fdf-eff6aed7f7ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), making this the most severe crackdown in the Islamic Republic's history.
Demonstrations have spread across all [31 provinces](https://substack.com/redirect/2892d2cd-eef4-4d12-8b92-de345597127f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including Kurdish regions where fears of separatism have long kept the regime on high alert. In response, Tehran has deployed the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) internally--a significant escalation, given that the IRGC is typically reserved for external threats and insurgency, not crowd control.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/12984ebb-d17c-454c-aa90-5638af206176?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The Iranian regime recognizes that these are not isolated and inconsequential demonstrations. That reality has already reshaped U.S. policy. President Trump has broken off talks with the current Iranian regime and warned that further violence against protesters could trigger U.S. strikes. The USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group has left the South China Sea to head towards the Middle East.
The United States has also begun evacuating personnel from its largest Middle East installation, the Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar. That base is widely viewed as a likely target in any Iranian retaliation scenario, and preemptive evacuation signals that planners now see elevated risk of escalation.
This is not just a moral or political crisis. It's also an economic one.
Iran is the world's fourth-largest OPEC exporter and a structural pillar of global energy flows with roughly a quarter of global seaborne oil moving through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's political stability is a defining factor upstream of commodity, shipping, insurance, and energy prices.
## **This Matters Far Beyond Iran 's Borders**
The Islamic Republic functions not just as a state, but as a hub of a regional network of proxies built to project power across the region. For decades, Tehran has relied on aligned groups--from Hamas in Gaza and Hezbollah in Lebanon to the Houthis in Yemen and militias embedded across Iraq and Syria--to deter adversaries, pressure rivals, and shift conflict away from Iranian territory.
The economic repercussions have also rippled across the world.
## **Geopolitics Is Now a Core Operating Risk**
In 2025, attacks by Iran-aligned Houthi forces on commercial shipping in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb forced a large share of global container traffic to reroute thousands of miles around Africa, driving up freight, fuel, and insurance costs. Airlines also rerouted flights to avoid expanding drone and missile threat corridors. Energy traders followed, embedding chokepoint risk as a standing condition of doing business.
For multinational companies, this has changed the basic math of exposure. The problem is no longer whether a firm operates inside Iran, but whether any part of its supply chain, shipping route, or insurance coverage passes through a region touched by Iran's reach. Events unfolding hundreds of miles away now regularly disrupt contracts, delay deliveries, or spike costs overnight. That is why companies tied to energy, logistics, and trade increasingly operate in a constant state of geopolitical stress--one where instability in Tehran ripples outward into the global economy.
Oil markets are already responding. Brent and WTI futures have become increasingly sensitive to political stress inside Iran as well as the likelihood of sanctions hardening further. Traders are pricing the possibility that sustained turmoil could eventually disrupt Iran's crude flows, even if production remains steady for now.
This feeds directly into gasoline prices, power costs, freight rates, and food prices.

For central banks hoping to engineer a soft landing, this is an unwelcome form of inflationary pressure. Higher energy costs driven by instability rather than demand cannot be solved with interest rates. You cannot rate-cut or quantitatively ease your way out of civil unrest in the Persian Gulf.
## **Sanctions Have Shifted From Targeted Pressure to Systemic Shock**
The policy implications of this moment are just as destabilizing as the unrest itself. President Trump's proposed 25 percent tariff on any country doing business with Iran would not isolate Tehran so much as entangle much of the global economy. More than 140 countries still maintain some level of trade with Iran, often limited to humanitarian goods or unavoidable commercial ties.
In practice, this shifts the burden from Iranian firms to entire supply chains. U.S. partners such as Japan, South Korea, India, and the UAE face a blunt dilemma: maintain limited engagement with Iran and risk punitive tariffs from Washington, or sever ties altogether and absorb economic pain for a crisis they did not create. Secondary sanctions once aimed at bad actors. Increasingly, they now threaten allies.
## **Venezuela 's Energy Impact**
Venezuela offers a revealing parallel to Iran.

In both places, the possibility of regime change hangs in the air. But the economic consequences of that instability could not be more different.
The most immediate beneficiaries of Venezuela's reopening are not oil producers but refiners, particularly along the U.S. Gulf Coast. Companies like Valero and Marathon built their facilities decades ago and are capable of handling Venezuela's heavy crude. That oil is cheaper, harder to process, and perfectly suited to plants that already have the equipment.
For refiners, this is a margin story: better inputs without new capital spending or political exposure on the ground. Consumers may see modest relief at the pump--especially for diesel and jet fuel--but Venezuela still accounts for roughly one percent of global supply. This is an outcome that will unfold over decades, not months, or even years.
The deeper effects are geopolitical.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/9f6a322e-fec9-405e-84b4-dde7ede48c6a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

The U.S. just seized the [6th oil tanker](https://substack.com/redirect/15f6a2cf-07a0-48b2-a390-fc0ce882d909?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), while [25 tankers re-registered under Russian flags](https://substack.com/redirect/ccf87c4a-5bf2-4a0d-bbf9-949cc909b82a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), 16 of these tankers were already under U.S. sanctions.

As Venezuelan barrels reenter the market, Canadian heavy-oil producers risk losing U.S. market share. China, which absorbed discounted Venezuelan crude during years of sanctions, loses leverage. Cuba becomes more exposed. India gains optionality away from Russian supply. Every additional barrel rearranges relationships, even if it barely moves prices.
That is why this moment feels so unsettled. Iran's unrest is pushing a persistent risk premium into energy markets, while Venezuela's tentative reopening offers selective relief without restoring confidence. Markets are being pulled in opposite directions--by fear on one side and constrained supply promise on the other.

Whether Iran's unrest hardens into regime change or Venezuela's reopening delivers lasting reform remains uncertain. What is already clear is that instability itself now carries a price--and the world is paying it in real time.

What is already clear is that instability itself now carries a price--and the world is paying it in real time. The Secretary's SitRep is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You 're currently a free subscriber to From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy. Subject: Thank you! **_Dear House Democrats – you make me proud to be a Democrat._**

Your fight to IMPEACH Trump’s stooges Pete Hegseth and RFK Jr. is exactly what our country needs right now.

The American people are sick and tired of Hegseth and RFK Jr.’s inexperience, dangerous lies, and life-threatening policies!

**This news gives me hope that we WILL hold them accountable — and stand up to Trump’s authoritarianism once and for all.**

**I’m so grateful to have you leading the way!**

[**That’s why I’m signing this card and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my appreciation and to help House Democrats FLIP the few seats we need to take back the majority »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/101442?t=9&akid=22648%2E878179%2EPU585A) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/101442?t=10&akid=22648%2E878179%2EPU585A)
