name: Shahid Buttar
subject: When will America finally heed MLK?
body:
A 20th century prophet venerated as a "civil rights hero" tried to warn this country generations ago of the crises our leaders have chosen to invite, rather than avert

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..JFVmZ4MuceVzXF7bEOwxK5Q9h4rBt74LZ9bDDRh-j-w?) for more
---
[](https://substack..PTkv561jP11ycJBXn0m4FgQrGhGdrfJF_pRqLrpt4nM?)
---
# [When will America finally heed MLK?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=185041000&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDQxMDAwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzM5NTQsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNTk1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7OA9eielcAkzX5Ip9sj41fbhOomVCI1umtE75tKDNoE)
### A 20th century prophet venerated as a "civil rights hero" tried to warn this country generations ago of the crises our leaders have chosen to invite, rather than avert
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Jan 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=185041000&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDQxMDAwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2ODgzMzk1NCwiZXhwIjoxNzcxNDI1OTU0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.RXE92zJepz6Sy0zT9bnfBkIi5AP4Cfg9Brg9sXAGw-Y&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=185041000&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDQxMDAwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzM5NTQsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNTk1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7OA9eielcAkzX5Ip9sj41fbhOomVCI1umtE75tKDNoE&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=185041000&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDQxMDAwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzM5NTQsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyNTk1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7OA9eielcAkzX5Ip9sj41fbhOomVCI1umtE75tKDNoE)
---
| | [](https://substack..r3EtnjMSH3XbvvqVcji8pNcMPzxenwpSMh60aWQAlv8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/when-will-america-finally-heed-mlk?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Celebrating the legacy of Martin Luther King, Jr. requires recalling not only his memory, but also his teachings. Despite having been elevated to the status of national icon, with a national holiday dedicated to his memory and a monument in the nation's capital, he remains widely misunderstood and underappreciated.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5b428e00-4617-4a17-907f-a79e0bac8f30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **Remembering a hero**
I wrote on last year's holiday about the perverse irony of Donald Trump returning to Washington on the same day. The juxtaposition could not possibly be more jarring.
## [MLK rolls in his grave, while Trump's return to Washington offers a few early surprises](https://substack.com/redirect/0b215e73-6e13-46ac-9f88-4d3b03347d3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| January 20, 2025
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0b215e73-6e13-46ac-9f88-4d3b03347d3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The juxtaposition of today's holiday dedicated to the late Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. with the re-inauguration of President Donald Trump could not offer a more compelling contrast or irony.](https://substack.com/redirect/0b215e73-6e13-46ac-9f88-4d3b03347d3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/0b215e73-6e13-46ac-9f88-4d3b03347d3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication made possible by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..JG9KbiB7mOvgT8ohQaLTfH6-zz7MBKfb1o-SqavORaw?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=185041000)
In previous years, I've reflected on other themes raised by today's holiday. In 2023, I explored the gulf between how MLK is widely understood today and the reality of his life, struggle, defeat, and assassination. He shared a clear analysis of the rot within America's political soul that could have informed a brighter future had America heeded his voice.
## [Reclaiming the radical legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.](https://substack.com/redirect/80a6ba45-2565-476f-b522-580e002637d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/10904314-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| January 16, 2023
[](https://substack.com/redirect/80a6ba45-2565-476f-b522-580e002637d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[[This post is also accessible as a podcast]](https://substack.com/redirect/80a6ba45-2565-476f-b522-580e002637d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/80a6ba45-2565-476f-b522-580e002637d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In 2024, I examined the prescience of King's warnings, focusing on the "intersecting evils" that he specifically identified: racism, capitalism, and militarism. Beyond offering a tragically timeless description of our country's criminal history, he also named the particular factors that drove the accelerating global climate catastrophe.
We live today in the shadow of sustained failures to act on his warnings.
## [Martin Luther King, Jr. tried to warn us](https://substack.com/redirect/a193ee91-984e-43b6-8d71-0f6ac1d917ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| January 15, 2024
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a193ee91-984e-43b6-8d71-0f6ac1d917ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Few figures in U.S. history are as widely recognized as the late Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. But while every American has heard his name, and most celebrate today's annual holiday commemorated in his honor, disturbingly few realize the profundity of his remarkable legacy](https://substack.com/redirect/a193ee91-984e-43b6-8d71-0f6ac1d917ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/a193ee91-984e-43b6-8d71-0f6ac1d917ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Martin Luther King, Jr. was a lot more than merely a "civil rights hero." He was a visionary, a prophet, and a patriot with a legacy as prolific as any of the founding Fathers. Few appreciate the depth of his profundity, or how much misery the future could have been saved had white America listened to MLK rather than reduce him to a caricature.
### **The contemporary relevance of history**
In the wake of Renee Good's recent murder in Minneapolis by ICE agent Jonathan Ross, I wrote about the disturbing continuity between this era of wanton state violence and the era of racial terror across the South invited by the end of Reconstruction.
## [Taking off the mask](https://substack.com/redirect/7fecae9e-6e1e-4ddc-ad05-e643517b5ca8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| Jan 17
[](https://substack.com/redirect/7fecae9e-6e1e-4ddc-ad05-e643517b5ca8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Last Wednesday, 37-year old Renee Good was shot in the face by Jonathan Ross, a federal ICE agent who--like any number of his colleagues--has been effectively empowered to stand above the law and disregard basic human and civil rights.](https://substack.com/redirect/7fecae9e-6e1e-4ddc-ad05-e643517b5ca8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/7fecae9e-6e1e-4ddc-ad05-e643517b5ca8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
There was no time of peace in the United States before Trump came to office. Immigrants have been forced to live in fear for decades. Black Americans have remained subjected to arbitrary and lethal state violence for centuries.
And the white establishment tolerated those injustices because--as King wrote--figures who enjoy social privileges want not to threaten their own positions.
The shoe is not on the _other_ foot today, so much as _both_ feet: the same communities long subjected to the whims of those in power have been joined by others who may have thought themselves immune from their struggles. There can be no pretense anymore about the illusion of separateness.
MLK told us that "injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere." The last half century--and the last year, in particular--has proven his point over and over again.
A poem I included at the end of my previous post represented [my attempt to paraphrase](https://substack.com/redirect/5437ea28-194c-456d-8c41-e0e2a05e5bb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) this dynamic that King publicly lamented.
### **What time is it?**
Dr. King called us to embrace demands for long denied justice. But most of the goals he sought during his life were [never granted,](https://substack.com/redirect/38d4947a-9974-4583-af20-a181b6ad7a94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the few that were have been flipped on their heads.
Many Americans have been forced to come to grips with the reality of life in the U.S., and the unfinished work of making real the promises on which our nation was founded.
Never has there been a more urgent time to take up the work that Dr. King was not allowed to finish, to cultivate the beloved community that he envisioned, and to embrace the risks necessary to defend the future from the continuing predatory patterns of the past.

But most of the goals he sought during his life were and the few that were have been flipped on their heads. Many Americans have been forced to come to grips with the reality of life in the U.S., and the unfinished work of making real the promises on which our nation was founded. Never has there been a more urgent time to take up the work that Dr. King was not allowed to finish, to cultivate the beloved community that he envisioned, and to embrace the risks necessary to defend the future from the continuing predatory patterns of the past. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! Help inform --and inspire--your friends by sharing this post. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Shahid Buttar South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 ? ?

Peter, Dr. King dedicated his life to combating injustice and building a future grounded in equality and moral courage. His call for justice echoes powerfully now, as we face a sustained assault on the very rights he marched for right here in Georgia. The Trump administration, aided by allies like Burt Jones, is systematically working to undermine our voting rights and silence Georgians’ voices. They are creating barriers, spreading distrust, and turning the sacred right to vote into a political battleground. This is an affront to the dream Dr. King preached: **a nation where every person has an equal voice and an equal say in our democracy.** Georgia is on the frontlines of this struggle. We are the state where change is possible, where the legacy of the civil rights movement meets the urgency of today. But standing on the frontlines means nothing if we don’t stand together. Dr. King taught us that justice requires unity, courage, and relentless determination. It requires every single one of us locking arms and moving forward as one. Let’s honor Dr. King today by recommitting to this fight. We must organize, mobilize, and protect the right to vote — not just as a tribute to the past, but as a promise to generations to come. [Sign on and commit to defending the rights that Dr. King marched for.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123611681/596011153/-1842629026?source=DPG_EM_FR_011926_B1_MLKDAY&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNDM5OTg2Yy00NWY1LWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NoqJ4tWTA5a2EXWwSMCEqPhY8rkntd-fmh9KbcSSnM0=&emci=fb0d3772-2bf3-f011-8d4c-000d3a54b560&emdi=b439986c-45f5-f011-8d4c-0022482d279b&ceid=43566853) In solidarity, Charlie

name: Haley Stevens
subject: The legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I believe in our capacity for progress.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1975%2E49239%2E31H91b)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/12316?t=2&akid=1975%2E49239%2E31H91b)
---
null,

Today, on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day, I’m reflecting on Dr. King’s extraordinary legacy. And the unfinished work we must carry forward, together.

**Dr. King believed in a nation where** _**every**_**person is treated with dignity.** Where opportunity is not determined by the color of your skin or what neighborhood you’re from. Where justice isn't just a dream, but a practice.
This weekend, I had the honor of joining Michiganders in celebrating Dr. King's life – visiting churches, talking with small-business owners, and attending MLK Day events across our state.

It was powerful. It was grounding. And it reminded me of how much work we still have to do.

Because here in Michigan, and across the country, too many people – especially communities of color – still face real barriers.

But I believe in our capacity for progress. I have hope.
That’s why I launched the **Michigan Hope Agenda**. It’s a vision to break down barriers and build a state where _every_ person in _every_ community, especially communities of color, can thrive.
In short, it's a promise to:
* Deliver federal investment where it’s needed most
* Lower costs and expand access to health care and housing
* Create safer neighborhoods and stronger schools
* Invest in clean water, infrastructure, and the next generation of Michigan workers

**This isn’t just policy; it’s personal.** It's for the Michiganders I know and love.

And it’s my way to do my part to continue what Dr. King taught us: _that justice isn’t passive._ It’s something we build. Together. Community by community. Person by person.
I’m inspired by Dr. King, and I was proud to walk alongside so many Michiganders who are doing the work of justice every day – in classrooms, in congregations, in city halls, and in homes across this state.
Let’s keep going. Let’s stand together. **Let’s build a world Dr. King would be proud of.**

With grit and gratitude,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/12354?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1975%2E49239%2E31H91b) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/12316?t=4&akid=1975%2E49239%2E31H91b)
---

Thank you,
Haley

name: Shontel Brown
subject: The “dream” is under attack.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
Today, we must honor and commemorate the legacy of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123613242/596016223/-153530995?refcode=EM_C_260119MLK_N_SB_90DO_7_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYzI1Yjc4Mi00N2Y1LWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=puN2q7zaSOSFpyJ_sgFoaTRlpMYhOpZDmlYwmrMQKh0=) Peter,

Today, we honor and commemorate the legacy of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., a trailblazer for civil rights and justice for Black Americans.

However, the holiday is bittersweet this year.

What has been happening with ICE has been horrifying. The Trump administration has been flooding Black and Brown communities with under-trained and over-aggressive agents, inviting abuse, mistakes, and tragedies to occur.

**This violence has been fueled by a president who rejects equality and peddles in hate and fear.** Last week, Trump went on record to state that civil rights led to white people being “very badly treated” and doubled down on his worldview that equity has led to “reverse discrimination.”

Dr. King and many other civil rights leaders sacrificed and put their lives on the line to ensure every American has the ability to participate in our democracy, live free from police brutality, and be treated as an equal. But now, these values are under threat.

We cannot _for a moment_ take our fundamental rights for granted. The Republican Party has made it clear that they intend to reverse all of the progress that we have made and enact new obstacles in our fight for a more just and equitable world. It’s up to us to defend our rights and ensure that our nation moves forward, not backwards.

In Martin Luther King Jr.’s memory, let’s commit to continuing to bend the arc toward justice.

Together,

Shontel Brown | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?

His legacy, and the movement he catalyzed, underscore the profound impact that one person's determination – along with collective action – can have on our society. Today, I hope we can each reflect on Dr. King’s life and decide what positive change we can make in our own communities. We live in challenging times, and it's never been more important for each of us to create the positive change we seek. name: Elaine Luria
subject: Keys to the kingdom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elaine Luria for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123614295/596019958/794473033?refcode=logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDA3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YmZhNDFlZC00N2Y1LWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FSFA9DCZeBxzv_5iOnPl7PPQ3R6xqytnKke_QwQLztI=)

| | I grew up in Birmingham, Alabama, the city Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. once described as “the most thoroughly segregated city in the United States.” It was in Birmingham that Dr. King’s commitment to nonviolence was brutally tested with fire hoses and attack dogs and mace. And it was there that Dr. King wrote one of his most remarkable works, “Letter from a Birmingham Jail.” Dr. King understood that in a democracy, violence reveals weakness, not strength. It’s the last resort for those who know they’re fighting a losing battle; a knee-jerk response by oppressors and bullies who fear the power of the people. That was exactly what we saw on January 6th, folks: **weakness**. We saw a desperate man resort to bloodshed and violence to defy the will of the people. Like the brutality in Birmingham, January 6th did not achieve its goal. But our struggle for justice is far from over. There’s never been a better time to recall the courage of Dr. King and all of the men and women who faced down fire hoses in Birmingham for the cause of freedom. There's never been a better time to remember that **we hold the keys to the kingdom.** Some moments in American history demand unusual courage. This is one of those moments. Let’s meet it together. — Elaine Luria | | | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/123614296/596019959/-507177818?refcode=EM_BS_LUR_260119_FR_MLKDay_A0_na_U1_X1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDA3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YmZhNDFlZC00N2Y1LWYwMTEtOGQ0Yy0wMDIyNDgyZDI3OWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FSFA9DCZeBxzv_5iOnPl7PPQ3R6xqytnKke_QwQLztI=)
---

clean_body: | = | | I grew up in Birmingham, Alabama, the city Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. once described as “the most thoroughly segregated city in the United States.” It was in Birmingham that Dr. King’s commitment to nonviolence was brutally tested with fire hoses and attack dogs and mace. And it was there that Dr. King wrote one of his most remarkable works, “Letter from a Birmingham Jail.” Dr. King understood that in a democracy, violence reveals weakness, not strength. It’s the last resort for those who know they’re fighting a losing battle; a knee-jerk response by oppressors and bullies who fear the power of the people. That was exactly what we saw on January 6th, folks: weakness. We saw a desperate man resort to bloodshed and violence to defy the will of the people. Like the brutality in Birmingham, January 6th did not achieve its goal. But our struggle for justice is far from over. There’s never been a better time to recall the courage of Dr. King and all of the men and women who faced down fire hoses in Birmingham for the cause of freedom. There's never been a better time to remember that we hold the keys to the kingdom. Some moments in American history demand unusual courage. This is one of those moments. Let’s meet it together. — Elaine Luria | | | = | | We believe that emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, . name: Justice Democrats
subject: [Send a Message] Impeach DHS Sec. Kristi Noem NOW!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice Democrats
body:
Over and over, DHS Secretary Kristi Noem has defended and encouraged ICE agents’ racist, chaotic, and murderous behavior.
#
ICE is on a fascist rampage in Minnesota and across the country, Peter.
Over and over, **Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem has defended and encouraged ICE agents’ racist, chaotic, and murderous behavior.**
After ICE shot and killed Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis last week, Noem immediately smeared Good as a “domestic terrorist” and pushed a false narrative disproved by video and eyewitness testimony in an attempt to justify this murder.
She’s since sent even more ICE agents to Minnesota to terrorize communities, and is now saying _Americans everywhere must be ready to show their papers to prove their citizenship._
**[Congress has grounds to act, and they must impeach Noem immediately. Send a letter to press them to step up and do what’s right →](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140981?t=2&akid=37379%2E1203867%2EvauZkY)**
**[Take Action](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140981?t=3&akid=37379%2E1203867%2EvauZkY)**
Articles of impeachment have already been introduced against Noem for her obstruction of Congress, violation of public trust, and self-dealing. So far, **at least 80 House Democrats have signed on, including Justice Democrat Rep. Ilhan Omar of Minnesota’s 5th district.**
In Ilhan’s words, justice and accountability “begin with impeaching Kristi Noem and ensuring no federal agent can act as judge, jury, and executioner in our streets."
We know that simply sending a letter won’t be enough to rein in ICE, but it’s an important tool we can use to build public pressure on those in power. Adding your name will take you less than a minute, but thousands of signatures add up fast.
**It’s still Abolish ICE, and Noem’s impeachment is the first step.**
Impeachment hearings would bring evidence out into the open. Millions of Americans would see the full extent of ICE’s assault on our country. That’s how we build a congressional majority that will actually put a stop to ICE once and for all.
**[So please, Peter, send a letter to demand every single House Democrat vote to impeach Kristi Noem and investigate the full scope of abuses at DHS and ICE.](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140981?t=4&akid=37379%2E1203867%2EvauZkY)**
In solidarity,
Justice Democrats

name: Stop ICE via Our Revolution
subject: We are under siege
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

After Renee Nicole Good was murdered by an ICE agent in Minneapolis, the
Trump administration rushed to cover it up before her name was even
released, spreading lies and disinformation that the officer acted in self
defense.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1011&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]Headline: GOP goes all-out to prevent accountability fro agent who shot
Renee Nicole Good

Video footage of the killing tells a very different story, but now the FBI
has locked Minnesota officials out of the investigation. This is what zero
accountability looks like – and it won’t change until we stop sending
BILLIONS in our tax dollars to fuel these attacks on our communities.

It’s long past time Congress acts to stop ICE’s reign of terror – and NOW
is the moment to do it.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1012&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]Will you urgently send a message to Congress demanding it stop funding
ICE to terrorize our communities? Congress is watching the protests and
counting the calls. If enough of us speak out, we can force them to end
this nightmare.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1013&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]ADD YOUR NAME

ICE’s violence is escalating – by design.

In recent days, the Trump administration has doubled down, EXPANDING ICE’s
presence in Minnesota and across the country. Arrests and violence are
skyrocketing as heavily armed ICE officers grab people off the streets and
outside homes and schools and tackle members of the community.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1014&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]Headline: 'Like a military occupation': clashes rise with federal
agents in Minneapolis

One thing is clear: if we don’t stop this, the violence will only
increase.

The Trump administration is now in overdrive to keep the truth from the
American people. Noem and Trump are flooding the airwaves with lies –
blaming protesters, stoking chaos, spinning falsehoods to excuse their
brutality – all to enable and prolong ICE’s reign of fear.

If Congress doesn’t rein in ICE now, these abuses will only get worse. We
cannot let ICE get away with murdering our neighbors and calling it
“normal.” We cannot allow Trump’s agents to become an unchecked secret
police force inside our own cities.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1015&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]Email your members of Congress now and demand they DEFUND ICE
IMMEDIATELY. Your message will be delivered straight to your
representatives – together let’s make our call so deafening they can't
ignore us.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34944?source=email-20260119&t=1016&akid=10092%2E5885482%2ELaz6ZK ]ADD YOUR NAME

Renee Nicole Good deserved her life, her dignity, and her rights – not
government bullets and a cover-up. If we let this stand, we are complicit.
It stops now.

When we organize, we win,

Our Revolution

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Dr. Martin Luther King’s mission
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#] Team,

Today, we remember Dr. Martin Luther King Jr and the Civil Rights Movement.

Dr. King believed that every American deserved equal rights. He fought relentlessly for voting rights, labor rights, and civil liberties. He told us what America could be, and I believe in his dream. I’m determined to make his dream, and the dream of millions of Americans, a reality.

Honoring Dr. King’s life and legacy means continuing his work. Today, when extremists are trying to drive us apart, when truth is being distorted and erased, and when our civil rights are once again under threat, we must recommit ourselves to protecting and defending liberty for all Americans.

I know that if we take action together, if we stand up for each other together, our best days will be ahead.

In honor of Dr. King on this national day of service, I urge you to see this day not as a day off. Instead, let us all view today as an opportunity to honor Dr. King’s vision by committing ourselves to continuing his mission, to serve our communities and fight for a more just world.

Thank you,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: Trisha
subject: MLK Day under the 2nd Trump admin: From despair to progress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Solidarity forever. Dr. King once said, “The labor movement was the principal force that transformed misery and despair into hope and progress.” Those words still ring true today.
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001XvBc3bsHgRWXLyJfHoB0-w%3D&ch=005337f2-e4bf-11f0-bd14-0242b661c010&ca=d9621eca-a11a-4175-9fff-d05174498c9a) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.
---
---
| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BIS4GLD0Wkyg7vq-G2VqBp1W4U5HVrO4ursmYK2J4mjqqiRNyDlFTWWKzzapvEk61XbzeYTM5NOKggw72KWnMAwdmnV8CT-KGXi76CeCSFYBaT0ZuiLyeMko8pL9Y8_KN_ob15P5n1ivp6ZQxswniUM6d-HWIVq62aV0BzsneZTC950og_-aNKj9OW22AhVLA7jUTtGaqtWQzzTd6IoCdJ4EBLZa5YyN&c=IDT7bX3LYEZNwV70wwGxsiW7awmhHcHc8gJ2KF0zwSkbrgJiahecPg==&ch=_XxmNXBv6hGRJPYQx9JCVgInh8geYGH-_7kRQob0bUN6zYYJksHg6g==)
---
| Peter,
Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
It is a holiday honoring one of the most important figures in American history. A civil rights leader who helped bend the moral arc of this country toward justice for people oppressed simply because of the color of their skin.
But Dr. King's legacy does not stop there.
He was a fierce champion for working people and labor unions. He understood that racial justice and economic justice are inseparable. That dignity on the job is a civil right. That democracy does not mean much if working families are crushed while the wealthy and powerful take everything.That's why the historic march on Washington where he delivered his iconic "I have a Dream" speech was for Jobs and Freedom.
Dr. King once said, "The labor movement was the principal force that transformed misery and despair into hope and progress." Those words still ring true today.
And they feel painfully prescient in this moment.
Right now, we are living under an administration that is unleashing federal force with no oversight through masked, unidentified agents detaining people, shooting people, and surveilling us online. A president who openly attacks minority communities and even threatens to strip people of their citizenship. A president who is no friend to labor, attacking collective bargaining rights and gutting agencies like OSHA and the National Labor Relations Board, whose sole mission is to protect workers from abuse by the wealthy and the elite.
It can feel dark. Overwhelming. Even hopeless at times.
But on a day like today, Dr. King's legacy shines a light forward.
He reminds us that progress has never been handed down from the top. It has always been won by ordinary people standing together. Workers organizing. Communities refusing to be silent. People choosing courage over fear.
As Dr. King said, "We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope."
That hope is what fuels this campaign. It is why I am running. When I worked with America's unions at the national AFL-CIO, I fought tirelessly for working families. That fight is a relentless force behind our campaign.
My opponent Lauren Boebert has been enabling Trump's racist and anti-labor agenda. And that's why she is more vulnerable than she has ever been. Boebert is only in Congress because of 546 votes. In 2024 when I faced Boebert, our campaign outperformed VP Kamala Harris by nearly seven points. Now our campaign just outraised her by over a million dollars.
[If you are able, I am asking you to make a contribution of $25, $15 or whatever is meaningful to you today in honor of Dr. King's legacy. Your support helps us fight for workers, protect our communities, and keep pushing this country toward justice, dignity, and equality for all.](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001BIS4GLD0Wkyg7vq-G2VqBp1W4U5HVrO4ursmYK2J4mjqqiRNyDlFTWWKzzapvEk6ZwXesxbZ90a-5N2jYEddYIk-xeveNLtJZiFoWApIvyotY7-oPOknJbq6JEpT57JOhaf_VtyzRgAR5-4cUsNPVcKVb-lZd9JrkeDPSowAsa2DcVB_DQIHnAytFpg8qx3S4NJEuTjxt4FPJUmRjy4wSG9L2YiROYx9hSXWROzOgSg=&c=IDT7bX3LYEZNwV70wwGxsiW7awmhHcHc8gJ2KF0zwSkbrgJiahecPg==&ch=_XxmNXBv6hGRJPYQx9JCVgInh8geYGH-_7kRQob0bUN6zYYJksHg6g==)

# ["Please do not think we think you are not important, but...](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=185072382&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg1MDcyMzgyLCJpYXQiOjE3Njg4MzcwMjIsImV4cCI6MTc3MTQyOTAyMiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r49Wi_aNp-a9qtCY_7S_xDDMCX4Sp1GurLSQDqt-GJk)

| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)

**" Please do not think we think you are not important, but..."**
Every year, on Martin Luther King Day, I feel that it is important to to remember and talk about how real the fight for equality still is.
Three years ago, during my campaign for US Senate, I spent MLK Day in a mostly black town (92%) called Howardville. It's in Missouri's far southeast "[Bootheel](https://substack.com/redirect/31cead4a-29b6-4064-8b06-13f207854c01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" region, the same area as Hayti Heights,[ another all black Missouri town whose struggles with clean water and sewage I wrote about a few weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/756e05a7-2815-4d65-afed-998fa6ee6f7d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
In the spirit of Dr. King, my friend and Howardville resident, Vanessa Frazier, had gathered a group of other local residents together to figure out how they could protect their access to voting, which had recently taken a serious blow.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f3f44bcb-97aa-476b-bdfa-2aab45d49506?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_In Howardville, Missouri, with Vanessa Frazier (center, black hat)_
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.5LAN0sjTVoqUyGUI_ExQ7fChWhvPUeZjgxuzLaYCt_g?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=185072382)
You see, Vanessa, and everyone else who lived in Howardville, had received letters telling them that their polling places had been shut down.
The letters read: "Please do not think we think you are not important, but with only 263 registered voters in the precinct, **it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place** ..."
A third of polling places were closed in New Madrid County, a county that stretches across 700 square miles.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3f5b051c-4fd7-4e60-a267-87b90330dd01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Vanessa 's letter with some of her speaking notes_
It was another reminder that Dr. King's fight for the democratic _and_ economic empowerment of everyday people is far from over. With zero public input, Howardville residents were told they'd need to travel miles and miles just to vote -- as would hundreds of their other friends in nearby towns. At the meeting, many residents said that they or their neighbors don't drive and that the voting alternative for them was a joke: an option to vote by mail--_with a notary_ --which most people there said they had no access to and which added another layer of burden to voting.
People from all around the area showed up at Vanessa's Howardville MLK Day event to talk about how they were blindsided by this. Republican and Democrat. Black and white.

But mostly black. And overwhelmingly poor.
If you've ever seen the movie Braveheart, the leadership in Missouri followed the same strategy as the English king at the Battle of Falkirk, firing indiscriminately into an infantry melee to take out as many people as possible, regardless of their side:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c1556fcf-40b8-491b-a59d-e71543f3cf90?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_" Archers"
"I beg your pardon, Sire, but won't we hit our own troops?"
"Yes, but we'll hit theirs as well. We have reserves. Attack."_
The reasoning: the English king had more total troops than the Scots, so an equal depletion in numbers favored him because it increased the ratio in his favor. And besides, they were just poor commoners, so who cares?
Welcome to the strategy behind modern voter suppression.
A theme in Howardville that MLK day, a day we should have been celebrating equal rights and the legacy of Dr. King, was that the deck is still stacked against the Howardville community. Just like it's always been.
Here they were, in the 2020s, battling local leadership that thinks they are irrelevant, to try to retain access to _voting_. But that's not all they talked about. They talked about some attitudes never change. About growing up attending local segregated schools nearly _fifteen years_ after the landmark 1954 Brown vs. Board of Education ruling, because the local leadership of that era felt the same way the current leadership felt-- that they weren't equal.
One of those old segregated school buildings, now falling apart, is actually on the national register of historic places because it was such a late segregated school.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5c1086b6-2661-451e-95bf-d212f98108fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Howardville HS Historic Registry Sign_
Politicians have been obsessed with attacking DEI lately. Because it seems unfair. And it feels unfair.
But the real reason they obsess over it is because they don't want us to talk about something that's even more unfair: being born black in America.
The situations in Howardville and Hayti Heights aren't exceptions or outliers. They are indicative of a problem that a lot of people want us to forget or go back to ignoring.
A lot of the time we think of racism as big acts or overt actions. And while these exist, perhaps the deepest impact of race in America is that it works a lot like a casino. The odds of you having a good outcome in any given situation are always worse if you are a minority, particularly if you are black. And these small disadvantages compound over time.
Healthcare is a good example. Black infants are[ more than twice as likely to die](https://substack.com/redirect/cf496a8b-2af6-4c57-8c1d-24837bf1c5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as white infants. Black moms are[ more than twice as likely](https://substack.com/redirect/9a5d236a-f7eb-45aa-ab8c-d432f16381d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to die as a result of childbirth than anyone else. They're also[ more than twice as likely to die](https://substack.com/redirect/2bbf263c-869c-423e-b2e2-91305cafddb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from breast cancer. Your doctor is[ less likely to respond](https://substack.com/redirect/39bc7285-9fb9-4b47-9f97-0c62ee3ffaf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to you. Your provider is[ less likely to listen to you](https://substack.com/redirect/6e97c15d-35b0-4e8f-a1fa-2591c9cb26e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or prescribe needed medication. And even[ less likely to believe](https://substack.com/redirect/6bd3b942-0b6e-4db6-8472-cccfe2af043e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) what you tell them.
And forget about hiring. Just[ having a black name makes you significantly less likely](https://substack.com/redirect/b26477f3-0967-49ad-8246-643fccef493f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to get a call on a job application. Even crazier, white men _with_ criminal records are[ more likely to get a job application call-back](https://substack.com/redirect/1f53de11-e74e-4674-8f11-a0dbf6968f32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than black men _without_ criminal records. Does that sound fair?
That doesn't mean there aren't financially well-off black people. A lot of black Americans have worked very hard and used a lot of talent to overcome the odds. Many of them supported me on the campaign trail. Developers, business leaders, engineers, doctors, you name it. Giants in their fields. And a common theme in all of our discussions was that they just wanted the next generation to have fair odds.

Because they aren't examples that the system is equal. They have beaten the odds, just like some people who walk into a casino actually walk back out richer. It doesn't change the fact that, overall, the house wins.

This is something I've been on the better side of numerous times in my life.
When I was a young man I got pulled over quite a few times, for running stop signs, for speeding, and other things. Those tickets would have broken me if I had gotten them all, but despite being pulled over 10 times in a two year span (oops!), I only got one ticket.
In a multiyear study of more than 60 million traffic stops across 20 states, [Stanford University's Open Policing Project](https://substack.com/redirect/b83ae77a-b9d2-4c46-afeb-8f8df9787cae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that black drivers are not only more likely to be stopped than white drivers, but that black and Hispanic motorists are also more likely to be ticketed and have their cars searched for less cause than whites. There have even been reports of local authorities [freely discussing](https://substack.com/redirect/71b5f514-9155-40b3-8c1f-1953c1052657?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [making light of violence](https://substack.com/redirect/81b22e03-feb9-4ea4-a82f-43a930abce24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against black people, and actually[ advising recruits](https://substack.com/redirect/c98dafd8-e6fb-4ba9-8909-b0f18465bb0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to shoot young marijuana users if they're black.
On April 4, 2015, an unarmed black man named Walter Scott was shot in the back five times and killed in North Charleston by a police officer. Scott, who had been stopped for a burnt out tail-light but had spent time in jail for not paying child support, was fleeing from the officer when he was shot. I remember getting in an argument at the time with someone who said that a person gets what they deserve if they don't listen to the police.

I strongly disagreed. You see, when I was eighteen years old, my roommates and I lived on the fourth floor and watched a little too much David Letterman. Letterman used to toss random things off of his roof, which was super cool, so we started doing the same thing out of our window when no one was nearby. Bouncy balls, a broken chair, a watermelon... It was dumb. One day we went a little too far by stuffing a cereal box with toilet paper, lighting it on fire, and dropping it out into the snow. Someone called the cops and we were arrested.

The judge gave us each a "suspended imposition of sentence"- which meant that if we stayed out of trouble, the convictions would disappear.
Unfortunately, I was mischievous, and several months later, while exploring a subterranean steam tunnel, a police officer discovered me and yelled at me to stop. Not wanting to get caught trespassing, because it would put my suspended conviction onto my record, I ran. I know, I'm not painting the most flattering picture of my young adulthood...

But I ran. And I got away. And it never crossed my mind that running from the police could have led to my death like it did for Walter Scott.
And yet, for my best friend growing up and high school cross country teammate, Benyam, even now in 2026, he has to keep that in the back of his mind that _just running around his neighborhood_ could lead to his death. A couple of years ago, after a string of black men were shot while exercising, Benyam[ published his running-while-black checklist in the Washington Post](https://substack.com/redirect/e6e5cd68-ec9e-46ba-b96c-21652bac4ac3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
_Is my beard too thick?_
_Is it too dark outside?_
_Am I dressed like a runner? God forbid I wear mesh b-ball shorts and a loose T-shirt in the suburbs of Dallas._
_Then, while on the run, I have to consider:_
_Is my rap music too loud? Can they hear it?_
_Does my cap, worn backward, send the wrong message?_
_Do I open up my stride now or wait until I get out of the neighborhood and into the open roads so I don 't appear to be running away from something or someone?_
_How many times have I looked over my shoulder on this block? Does that look suspicious?_
_Then there is scenario planning while running -- if this, then that:_
_-If you see a young white woman on same path, cross street immediately and try not to make eye contact twice._
_-If there is a white older woman/man on same path, wave/nod/smile, cross street casually._
_-If you 're running behind them and they don't hear you, cough loudly/clear throat, cross street._
_-If the sidewalk is narrow, always give way to walkers or bikers and step into the grass or street, even though you 've shattered your ankle twice, once on grass and once stepping into the street from the sidewalk._
_-Turn down volume on music when passing by neighbors. Need to be able to respond if they say something -- don't want to come off profiled as angry black man._
_-If a raised pickup truck approaches, immediately take to the sidewalk and get a good look at driver. High alert. Ready to dive into someone 's yard in case he swerves into me._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d8773100-6087-4eac-b5b4-0dfb9058ad51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Benyam at my Marine Corps Officer Candidate School graduation, Quantico, Virginia_
I have never experienced what it's like to be black in this country. I only know what it was like to grow up white in a black area of Jefferson City, Missouri, and it's been eye opening for me to look back.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/52673cd3-3a5b-40d5-8bb9-b98d4f25ceb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bf1a0f22-a0bb-454e-99ad-417fb2b3c376?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_My sister and I on our back porch with the Mays ' kids and in front of our old house with Ms. Presberry, who moved to Jefferson City from Caruthersville in the Missouri bootheel, right next to Haiti Heights and Howardville_
And I don't just mean that city maintenance was neglected and that the neighborhood is now falling apart.
I mean the things that people don't say out loud or write about or pretend don't exist.
Things like people looking down on where I lived. Other kid's parents hesitating for them to come over. White kids calling my black neighbor kids the n-word consistently throughout my school years. High school classmates telling me that I lived in "porch monkey" land.
I remember being a 20-year-old Yale student back home over break and spending time on the other side of town with the fancy folks. Hanging with adults in high-up positions who oversaw hiring for their organizations and hearing them casually drop the n-word when referring to black people.
It's no surprise to me that people like that would interview white convicts for job interviews instead of black men without criminal records.
This isn't to say that everyone I interacted with held those views. The vast majority of people in my hometown were decent and cared about one another. But plenty were not, and that stacked the deck.

Being black now isn't really any different than it was then. The deck is still stacked, and it really isn't fair.

That's why this MLK Day, I'm hoping you will join me in celebrating some of the great black women leaders I met during my campaign who are fighting every day to get their communities the respect and voice they deserve.

Vanessa down in Howardville, who fought for her neighbors to have the right to vote. Mayor Catrina in Hayti Heights, who saved her city's water and sewage systems. Shalonda Webb, St. Louis County Councilwoman and the hardest working public servant I've ever met. And all the others across this great state who work hard every day, against the odds, to make life just a little bit better for everyone around them, no matter what color they are.
I hope they try even bigger things some day and even if the odds are against them, as they always have been, I'll be there for them and I hope you will be, too.
Lucas

clean_body: ...it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place." | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? ...it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place." | | | Jan 19 | | | | | | | | | | | | " Please do not think we think you are not important, but..." Every year, on Martin Luther King Day, I feel that it is important to to remember and talk about how real the fight for equality still is. Three years ago, during my campaign for US Senate, I spent MLK Day in a mostly black town (92%) called Howardville. It's in Missouri's far southeast "" region, the same area as Hayti Heights,. In the spirit of Dr. King, my friend and Howardville resident, Vanessa Frazier, had gathered a group of other local residents together to figure out how they could protect their access to voting, which had recently taken a serious blow. | | ---|---|--- In Howardville, Missouri, with Vanessa Frazier (center, black hat) Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You see, Vanessa, and everyone else who lived in Howardville, had received letters telling them that their polling places had been shut down. The letters read: "Please do not think we think you are not important, but with only 263 registered voters in the precinct, it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place ..." A third of polling places were closed in New Madrid County, a county that stretches across 700 square miles. | | ---|---|--- Vanessa 's letter with some of her speaking notes It was another reminder that Dr. King's fight for the democratic and economic empowerment of everyday people is far from over. With zero public input, Howardville residents were told they'd need to travel miles and miles just to vote -- as would hundreds of their other friends in nearby towns. At the meeting, many residents said that they or their neighbors don't drive and that the voting alternative for them was a joke: an option to vote by mail--with a notary --which most people there said they had no access to and which added another layer of burden to voting. People from all around the area showed up at Vanessa's Howardville MLK Day event to talk about how they were blindsided by this. Republican and Democrat. Black and white. But mostly black. And overwhelmingly poor. If you've ever seen the movie Braveheart, the leadership in Missouri followed the same strategy as the English king at the Battle of Falkirk, firing indiscriminately into an infantry melee to take out as many people as possible, regardless of their side: " Archers" "I beg your pardon, Sire, but won't we hit our own troops?" "Yes, but we'll hit theirs as well. We have reserves. Attack." The reasoning: the English king had more total troops than the Scots, so an equal depletion in numbers favored him because it increased the ratio in his favor. And besides, they were just poor commoners, so who cares? Welcome to the strategy behind modern voter suppression. A theme in Howardville that MLK day, a day we should have been celebrating equal rights and the legacy of Dr. King, was that the deck is still stacked against the Howardville community. Just like it's always been. Here they were, in the 2020s, battling local leadership that thinks they are irrelevant, to try to retain access to voting. But that's not all they talked about. They talked about some attitudes never change. About growing up attending local segregated schools nearly fifteen years after the landmark 1954 Brown vs. Board of Education ruling, because the local leadership of that era felt the same way the current leadership felt-- that they weren't equal. One of those old segregated school buildings, now falling apart, is actually on the national register of historic places because it was such a late segregated school. | | ---|---|--- Howardville HS Historic Registry Sign Politicians have been obsessed with attacking DEI lately. Because it seems unfair. And it feels unfair. But the real reason they obsess over it is because they don't want us to talk about something that's even more unfair: being born black in America. The situations in Howardville and Hayti Heights aren't exceptions or outliers. They are indicative of a problem that a lot of people want us to forget or go back to ignoring. A lot of the time we think of racism as big acts or overt actions. And while these exist, perhaps the deepest impact of race in America is that it works a lot like a casino. The odds of you having a good outcome in any given situation are always worse if you are a minority, particularly if you are black. And these small disadvantages compound over time. Healthcare is a good example. Black infants are as white infants. Black moms are to die as a result of childbirth than anyone else. They're also from breast cancer. Your doctor is to you. Your provider is or prescribe needed medication. And even what you tell them. And forget about hiring. Just to get a call on a job application. Even crazier, white men with criminal records are than black men without criminal records. Does that sound fair? That doesn't mean there aren't financially well-off black people. A lot of black Americans have worked very hard and used a lot of talent to overcome the odds. Many of them supported me on the campaign trail. Developers, business leaders, engineers, doctors, you name it. Giants in their fields. And a common theme in all of our discussions was that they just wanted the next generation to have fair odds. Because they aren't examples that the system is equal. They have beaten the odds, just like some people who walk into a casino actually walk back out richer. It doesn't change the fact that, overall, the house wins. This is something I've been on the better side of numerous times in my life. When I was a young man I got pulled over quite a few times, for running stop signs, for speeding, and other things. Those tickets would have broken me if I had gotten them all, but despite being pulled over 10 times in a two year span (oops!), I only got one ticket. In a multiyear study of more than 60 million traffic stops across 20 states, found that black drivers are not only more likely to be stopped than white drivers, but that black and Hispanic motorists are also more likely to be ticketed and have their cars searched for less cause than whites. There have even been reports of local authorities and against black people, and actually to shoot young marijuana users if they're black. On April 4, 2015, an unarmed black man named Walter Scott was shot in the back five times and killed in North Charleston by a police officer. Scott, who had been stopped for a burnt out tail-light but had spent time in jail for not paying child support, was fleeing from the officer when he was shot. I remember getting in an argument at the time with someone who said that a person gets what they deserve if they don't listen to the police. I strongly disagreed. You see, when I was eighteen years old, my roommates and I lived on the fourth floor and watched a little too much David Letterman. Letterman used to toss random things off of his roof, which was super cool, so we started doing the same thing out of our window when no one was nearby. Bouncy balls, a broken chair, a watermelon... It was dumb. One day we went a little too far by stuffing a cereal box with toilet paper, lighting it on fire, and dropping it out into the snow. Someone called the cops and we were arrested. The judge gave us each a "suspended imposition of sentence"- which meant that if we stayed out of trouble, the convictions would disappear. Unfortunately, I was mischievous, and several months later, while exploring a subterranean steam tunnel, a police officer discovered me and yelled at me to stop. Not wanting to get caught trespassing, because it would put my suspended conviction onto my record, I ran. I know, I'm not painting the most flattering picture of my young adulthood... But I ran. And I got away. And it never crossed my mind that running from the police could have led to my death like it did for Walter Scott. And yet, for my best friend growing up and high school cross country teammate, Benyam, even now in 2026, he has to keep that in the back of his mind that just running around his neighborhood could lead to his death. A couple of years ago, after a string of black men were shot while exercising, Benyam: Is my beard too thick? Is it too dark outside? Am I dressed like a runner? God forbid I wear mesh b-ball shorts and a loose T-shirt in the suburbs of Dallas. Then, while on the run, I have to consider: Is my rap music too loud? Can they hear it? Does my cap, worn backward, send the wrong message? Do I open up my stride now or wait until I get out of the neighborhood and into the open roads so I don 't appear to be running away from something or someone? How many times have I looked over my shoulder on this block? Does that look suspicious? Then there is scenario planning while running -- if this, then that: -If you see a young white woman on same path, cross street immediately and try not to make eye contact twice. -If there is a white older woman/man on same path, wave/nod/smile, cross street casually. -If you 're running behind them and they don't hear you, cough loudly/clear throat, cross street. -If the sidewalk is narrow, always give way to walkers or bikers and step into the grass or street, even though you 've shattered your ankle twice, once on grass and once stepping into the street from the sidewalk. -Turn down volume on music when passing by neighbors. Need to be able to respond if they say something -- don't want to come off profiled as angry black man. -If a raised pickup truck approaches, immediately take to the sidewalk and get a good look at driver. High alert. Ready to dive into someone 's yard in case he swerves into me. | | ---|---|--- Benyam at my Marine Corps Officer Candidate School graduation, Quantico, Virginia I have never experienced what it's like to be black in this country. I only know what it was like to grow up white in a black area of Jefferson City, Missouri, and it's been eye opening for me to look back. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- My sister and I on our back porch with the Mays ' kids and in front of our old house with Ms. Presberry, who moved to Jefferson City from Caruthersville in the Missouri bootheel, right next to Haiti Heights and Howardville And I don't just mean that city maintenance was neglected and that the neighborhood is now falling apart. I mean the things that people don't say out loud or write about or pretend don't exist. Things like people looking down on where I lived. Other kid's parents hesitating for them to come over. White kids calling my black neighbor kids the n-word consistently throughout my school years. High school classmates telling me that I lived in "porch monkey" land. I remember being a 20-year-old Yale student back home over break and spending time on the other side of town with the fancy folks. Hanging with adults in high-up positions who oversaw hiring for their organizations and hearing them casually drop the n-word when referring to black people. It's no surprise to me that people like that would interview white convicts for job interviews instead of black men without criminal records. This isn't to say that everyone I interacted with held those views. The vast majority of people in my hometown were decent and cared about one another. But plenty were not, and that stacked the deck. Being black now isn't really any different than it was then. The deck is still stacked, and it really isn't fair. That's why this MLK Day, I'm hoping you will join me in celebrating some of the great black women leaders I met during my campaign who are fighting every day to get their communities the respect and voice they deserve. Vanessa down in Howardville, who fought for her neighbors to have the right to vote. Mayor Catrina in Hayti Heights, who saved her city's water and sewage systems. Shalonda Webb, St. Louis County Councilwoman and the hardest working public servant I've ever met. And all the others across this great state who work hard every day, against the odds, to make life just a little bit better for everyone around them, no matter what color they are. I hope they try even bigger things some day and even if the odds are against them, as they always have been, I'll be there for them and I hope you will be, too. name: Team Tex
subject: WATCH: Our First TV Ad — Faith. Family. America First.
body:
Check it out

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=778c8ea011&e=2814cf23ed)
| [WATCH: Mark Teixeira’s First TV Ad!](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=cbf88da539&e=2814cf23ed)
---
| | Peter,
We’re excited to announce that our [**first television ad**](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=48d8f7b014&e=2814cf23ed)**** of the campaign is on the air introducing **Mark “Tex” Teixeira** — a Christian, husband, father, and conservative **political outsider** Texas needs in Congress.
Raised by a teacher and a U.S. veteran, Mark was taught early to put **faith first** , work hard, and serve others. Those values shaped his success in Major League Baseball and business — and that’s why he’s running to serve Texas’ 21st Congressional District.
In Congress, Mark will **stand with President Trump** to:
* **Secure the border** and deport criminal illegal immigrants
* **Crush drug cartels** and put public safety first
* **Protect religious freedom** and Christian values
* **Defend women’s sports** and common sense

Mark is proud to be endorsed by **Governor Greg Abbott** , **Jim Jordan** , **Riley Gaines** , **National Border Patrol Council** , True Texas Project, Texas College Republicans, along with more than 100 conservative leaders across TX-21.
👉 [**Watch and share our new TV ad today**](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=18fa07fc35&e=2814cf23ed)**.**
Thank you for standing with us as we fight for Texas families and America First.
— **Team Mark “Tex” Teixeira** 🇺🇸 America First ConservativeTexas’ 21st Congressional District
---
| [Watch and Share Our New TV Ad >>](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=15449e5d2c&e=2814cf23ed)
---
|
---
| |
_**What They’re Saying About The Teixeira Campaign
**_
**TEXAS GOVERNOR GREG ABBOTT**
“Mark “Tex” Teixeira has proven his commitment to Texas values, fighting for conservative principles and supporting Republican leaders. He’ll stand strong for border security, economic freedom, and the America First agenda Texans deserve in Washington.”

**NATIONAL BORDER PATROL COUNCIL**
”Teixeira represents a new generation of leadership who will stand with our agents, secure the border, and fight for the men and women on the front lines.”

**RILEY GAINES, LEADING WOMEN’S ADVOCATE**
“I’ve been fortunate to get to know Mark Teixeira, and it’s clear he has a deep love for his family, his Christian faith, and our country.”
**TRUE TEXAS PROJECT** “One conversation with Mark Teixeira made it clear his vision for America matches ours... and he can go about it without any strings attached."
**TEXAS COLLEGE REPUBLICANS**
"The Texas College Republicans are proud to endorse someone with those values that put Texans first."

**SUE PINER, FORMER COMAL COUNTY GOP CHAIRMAN**
“In the open seat for TX-21, Mark Teixeira stands out as a political outsider and a breath of fresh air.”

**ANNE NEWMAN, FORMER KENDALL COUNTY REPUBLICAN CLUB PRESIDENT**
“Mark has a servant’s heart who puts principles over politics. He wants to serve our country, and he will fight for We the People.”

**GEORGE RODRIGUEZ, SAN ANTONIO CONSERVATIVE LEADER**
“I’ve spent decades in the political trenches fighting for the conservative cause, and I could not be more excited to support Mark Teixeira for Congress.”

**CONGRESSMAN BRANDON GILL, HOUSE FREEDOM CAUCUS**
“Mark Teixeira is a true America First patriot. We can count on him to work with President Trump to fully secure the border, and he will stand with us to hold government accountable to the people.”
---
|
---
| | | | [|
---](https://teixeiraforcongress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=aaeca7d0d6f0c241ad9d38cc7&id=93b4c0aacc&e=2814cf23ed)

name: Adam Schiff
subject: I cannot emphasize the importance of this enough -
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi -- it's Adam Schiff, DSCC Vice Chair.

**In a moment, I'm going to talk to you about something deeply important, both to me personally and to the future of our nation. But first, I have to ask:[_Will you chip in $5 right now to help flip the Senate blue and take power away from the MAGA majority as they work to enact Trump's dangerous agenda?_](https://go.dscc.org/go/22370?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=22435%2E9716520%2EVZqkc9)**

I'm going to put it plain and simple -- **Democrats have a huge opportunity to pick up Senate seats -- including in a pair of special elections on the calendar.** We've got a chance to capture seats that wouldn't have been up for years -- and in this moment, after all that we've seen from Donald Trump and his lawless administration, we have to fight for every opportunity to defeat them.

As someone who has won a special election to the Senate, I cannot emphasize enough the importance of this. Democrats only need a few seats to flip the Senate majority, and with the GOP defending more seats than they were expecting, we have a real chance to catch them flat-footed.

But we can't just sit around and cross our fingers -- **we must do what we can right now to secure the resources we need to win seats like Ohio and Florida, defend blue battleground seats across the country, pick up key seats, and defeat Trump's handpicked enablers.**

**[These special elections are among a tiny handful of races that could decide the majority, so I have to ask: Will you chip in $5 to help Democrats win key elections like these and flip the Senate majority? In critical races, every dollar counts.](https://go.dscc.org/go/22370?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=22435%2E9716520%2EVZqkc9)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

| | [Donate $5](https://go.dscc.org/go/22370?amount=5&t=7&amount=5&express_lane=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20260119-AdamSchiff-SchiffSpecialElection-Pros-sl1&akid=22435%2E9716520%2EVZqkc9)

clean_body: | | | Hi -- it's Adam Schiff, DSCC Vice Chair. In a moment, I'm going to talk to you about something deeply important, both to me personally and to the future of our nation. But first, I have to ask: I'm going to put it plain and simple -- Democrats have a huge opportunity to pick up Senate seats -- including in a pair of special elections on the calendar. We've got a chance to capture seats that wouldn't have been up for years -- and in this moment, after all that we've seen from Donald Trump and his lawless administration, we have to fight for every opportunity to defeat them. As someone who has won a special election to the Senate, I cannot emphasize enough the importance of this. Democrats only need a few seats to flip the Senate majority, and with the GOP defending more seats than they were expecting, we have a real chance to catch them flat-footed. But we can't just sit around and cross our fingers -- we must do what we can right now to secure the resources we need to win seats like Ohio and Florida, defend blue battleground seats across the country, pick up key seats, and defeat Trump's handpicked enablers. We need your help to fight for the Senate. name: Seth Magaziner
subject: Dr. King’s work is not finished
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress
body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=Apfc9S3P%2FpUvotxvmnLG84wKfdPot0l418cDEygAadggnYJQMsLTIbGRgiaLMmUyQZPZiWd4SCPxe4SukPeLJ2cR6TKPEaA3ikUJ8OyYAr6JtdAoN5h6D09SnEpfyJA%2FNIJi%2FdL9wjgFWnVqTZ3Jzc6aq8wtrCq9AfjdNyVEK8f3np62iPSktBZtcc6tllhPcjG6STwyM2ib4yy10AxjnhxZZVd%2FCT4g4kdYf02OyonbGhbvDZzjbKahXE2sSNt023uYIw3ideoj46Ku9O4LLgrbc6lCWafKQXhBTsGAhta5vVzM9Zg%2BxxSLleNoFimhfeq4WQqmOYxv8RqZ4UFTyBqoY%2BE6NjifX5xoCQlVY7A8qurOoD%2FYDxkpde58pedYnAsQFkIWfvZ6%2FmY1ruf7tr%2Bmv8ucx1Z9ElPwgReq6EVPll1qSxcnJkL2YjUDoS0P)
---
| |
---
Peter,

Today we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., not just by remembering his words, but by reflecting on the work he left unfinished.

Dr. King fought for civil rights, voting rights, labor rights, and economic equality at a time when doing so came with real personal risk. He understood that democracy only works when it is inclusive, and that freedom is meaningless if it isn’t paired with fairness, dignity, and equal protection under the law.

That legacy matters deeply today. Across the country, we are seeing renewed efforts to roll back voting access, weaken civil rights protections and due process, and undermine the progress generations fought to achieve. The erosion of civil rights happens slowly, through policy decisions and silence that allow injustice to take root.

Honoring Dr. King means refusing to accept that erosion as inevitable. It means standing up for the right to vote, for equal treatment under the law, and for a government that serves everyone, not just the powerful or well-connected.

**In Congress, I take that responsibility seriously. I will continue fighting to protect civil rights, expand opportunity, and uphold the democratic values Dr. King dedicated his life to advancing.**

Today, and every day, we owe it to the next generation to keep that work moving forward.

— Seth

subject: To those searching for a way forward:

name: Roy Cooper
subject: I know how to win a swing state
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Will you join me in the fight?
**Winning in a swing state isn’t easy, Peter.**
_Not when the other side is throwing everything they’ve got at you._
_Not when President Trump makes you a personal target._
**That’s where things stand here in North Carolina ahead of the midterm elections.**
Our election is the most competitive U.S. Senate race anywhere in the country — it’s the one that will determine whether or not we can take back the Senate for the people and break the GOP’s one-party hold on Washington.
President Trump and his political allies are targeting me. Far-right PACs and mega donors are lined up to pour hundreds of millions into defeating me. _But I know that we can win._
You see, I’ve won some of the most competitive statewide races here in battleground NC. In fact, _I have never lost a race._ Over and over, we’ve won _together_ — and that’s how we’ll win again.
**[Far-right Republicans are terrified that I’m the Democrat who can flip this seat from red to blue for the first time since 2008. I’m asking you to donate to help me prove that we can. Split any amount between our campaign and Heartland Patriots to join me in the fight.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99560?refcode=em20260119-9944&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9944%2E614464%2EEnZCRU)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/99560?refcode=em20260119-9944&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9944%2E614464%2EEnZCRU)**
Appreciate your help,
Roy Cooper

| [](https://www.facebook.com/lucaskuncemo/) | [](https://twitter.com/lucaskuncemo) | [](https://www.instagram.com/lucaskuncemo/)

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida don’t stick to the status quo
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260119-1831-foote&t=1001&akid=1831%2E342411%2Ef6H1vJ ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida on the front lines every day,
fighting for our communities.

They don’t stick with the status quo. They stand up, speak truth to power,
and fight for justice on all fronts — economic, social, racial, and more.

The Squad is fighting for a democracy where working people have a voice,
not just the ultra-wealthy and well-connected.

Here’s the truth: Trump and his billionaire buddies are on the attack.
From spreading lies and hateful rhetoric about our progressive champions
to trying to silence our movement, they’ll stop at nothing.

But we’re not backing down. The Squad Victory Fund doesn’t take corporate
PAC money. We never have, and we never will. Every outreach effort, every
fight for justice — it’s fueled by grassroots supporters like you.

That’s why we’re asking:

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20260119-movement.Iz54LN&refcode=em20260119-1831&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1831%2E342411%2Ef6H1vJ ]Will you pitch in $3 today to keep this movement strong?
Your support gives the Squad the resources to push back against far-right
extremists, amplify the voices of working people, and build a future that
works for everyone.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com
